Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
    1: /**
    2:  * CLI: memorytree discover 鈥?scan and import local transcripts.
    3:  * Port of scripts/discover-transcripts.py
    4:  */
    5: 
    6: import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    7: import { basename, resolve } from 'node:path'
    8: 
    9: import { CLIENTS, slugify } from '../transcript/common.js'
   10: import { defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot, discoverSourceFiles, inferProjectSlug, transcriptMatchesRepo } from '../transcript/discover.js'
   11: import { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '../transcript/import.js'
   12: import { parseTranscript } from '../transcript/parse.js'
   13: import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
   14: 
   15: export interface DiscoverOptions {
   16:   root: string
   17:   client: string
   18:   scope: string
   19:   projectName: string
   20:   globalRoot: string
   21:   rawUploadPermission: string
   22:   limit: number
   23:   format: string
   24: }
   25: 
   26: export async function cmdDiscover(options: DiscoverOptions): Promise<number> {
   27:   const root = resolve(options.root)
   28:   if (!existsSync(root)) {
   29:     process.stderr.write(`root does not exist: ${root}\n`)
   30:     return 1
   31:   }
   32: 
   33:   const repoSlug = slugify(options.projectName.trim() || basename(root), 'project')
   34:   const globalRoot = options.globalRoot ? resolve(options.globalRoot) : defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot()
   35:   const requestedClients = options.client === 'all' ? CLIENTS : new Set([options.client])
   36:   const discovered = discoverSourceFiles(requestedClients)
   37: 
   38:   const results: Record<string, unknown>[] = []
   39:   const skipped: Record<string, string>[] = []
   40: 
   41:   for (const [client, source] of discovered) {
   42:     if (options.limit > 0 && results.length >= options.limit) break
   43: 
   44:     let parsed
   45:     try {
   46:       parsed = parseTranscript(client, source)
   47:     } catch (err: unknown) {
   48:       skipped.push({
   49:         client,
   50:         source,
   51:         reason: 'parse-error',
   52:         error: summarizeException(err),
   53:       })
   54:       continue
   55:     }
   56: 
   57:     const candidateProject = inferProjectSlug(parsed)
   58:     const matchesCurrentRepo = transcriptMatchesRepo(parsed, root, repoSlug)
   59: 
   60:     if (!transcriptHasContent(parsed)) {
   61:       skipped.push({ client, source, reason: 'no-importable-content', project: candidateProject })
   62:       continue
   63:     }
   64: 
   65:     if (options.scope === 'current-project' && !matchesCurrentRepo) {
   66:       skipped.push({ client, source, reason: 'unrelated-project', project: candidateProject })
   67:       continue
   68:     }
   69: 
   70:     try {
   71:       const result = await importTranscript(
   72:         parsed,
   73:         root,
   74:         globalRoot,
   75:         matchesCurrentRepo ? repoSlug : candidateProject,
   76:         matchesCurrentRepo ? options.rawUploadPermission : 'not-applicable',
   77:         matchesCurrentRepo,
   78:       )
   79:       results.push({ ...result, matches_current_repo: matchesCurrentRepo })
   80:     } catch (err: unknown) {
   81:       skipped.push({
   82:         client,
   83:         source,
   84:         reason: 'import-error',
   85:         project: candidateProject,
   86:         error: summarizeException(err),
   87:       })
   88:     }
   89:   }
   90: 
   91:   const payload = {
   92:     repo: toPosixPath(root),
   93:     repo_project: repoSlug,
   94:     scope: options.scope,
   95:     client_filter: options.client,
   96:     discovered_count: discovered.length,
   97:     imported_count: results.length,
   98:     repo_mirror_count: results.filter(r => r['matches_current_repo']).length,
   99:     global_only_count: results.filter(r => !r['matches_current_repo']).length,
  100:     skipped_count: skipped.length,
  101:     imports: results,
  102:     skipped,
  103:   }
  104: 
  105:   if (options.format === 'json') {
  106:     process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(payload) + '\n')
  107:   } else {
  108:     process.stdout.write(formatDiscoverText(payload) + '\n')
  109:   }
  110:   return 0
  111: }
  112: 
  113: function formatDiscoverText(payload: Record<string, unknown>): string {
  114:   const lines = [
  115:     `repo: ${payload['repo'] ?? ''}`,
  116:     `repo_project: ${payload['repo_project'] ?? ''}`,
  117:     `scope: ${payload['scope'] ?? ''}`,
  118:     `client_filter: ${payload['client_filter'] ?? ''}`,
  119:     `discovered_count: ${payload['discovered_count'] ?? 0}`,
  120:     `imported_count: ${payload['imported_count'] ?? 0}`,
  121:     `repo_mirror_count: ${payload['repo_mirror_count'] ?? 0}`,
  122:     `global_only_count: ${payload['global_only_count'] ?? 0}`,
  123:     `skipped_count: ${payload['skipped_count'] ?? 0}`,
  124:   ]
  125: 
  126:   const imports = payload['imports']
  127:   if (Array.isArray(imports) && imports.length > 0) {
  128:     lines.push('imports:')
  129:     for (const item of imports.slice(0, 10)) {
  130:       const rec = item as Record<string, unknown>
  131:       lines.push(`- ${rec['client']} ${rec['project']} ${rec['session_id']} repo_mirror=${String(rec['matches_current_repo']).toLowerCase()}`)
  132:     }
  133:     if (imports.length > 10) lines.push(`- ... ${imports.length - 10} more`)
  134:   }
  135:   return lines.join('\n')
  136: }
  137: 
  138: function summarizeException(err: unknown): string {
  139:   if (err instanceof Error) return err.message.trim() || err.constructor.name
  140:   return String(err)
  141: }

Using Node v20.20.1
    1: /**
    2:  * CLI: memorytree import 鈥?import one transcript file.
    3:  * Port of scripts/import-transcripts.py
    4:  */
    5: 
    6: import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    7: import { basename, resolve } from 'node:path'
    8: 
    9: import { slugify } from '../transcript/common.js'
   10: import { defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot, inferProjectSlug, transcriptMatchesRepo } from '../transcript/discover.js'
   11: import { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '../transcript/import.js'
   12: import { inferClient, parseTranscript } from '../transcript/parse.js'
   13: 
   14: export interface ImportOptions {
   15:   root: string
   16:   source: string
   17:   client: string
   18:   projectName: string
   19:   globalRoot: string
   20:   rawUploadPermission: string
   21:   format: string
   22:   forceRepo: boolean
   23: }
   24: 
   25: export async function cmdImport(options: ImportOptions): Promise<number> {
   26:   const root = resolve(options.root)
   27:   const source = resolve(options.source)
   28: 
   29:   if (!existsSync(root)) {
   30:     process.stderr.write(`root does not exist: ${root}\n`)
   31:     return 1
   32:   }
   33:   if (!existsSync(source)) {
   34:     process.stderr.write(`source transcript does not exist: ${source}\n`)
   35:     return 1
   36:   }
   37: 
   38:   const client = inferClient(options.client, source)
   39:   const globalRoot = options.globalRoot ? resolve(options.globalRoot) : defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot()
   40:   const repoSlug = slugify(options.projectName.trim() || basename(root), 'project')
   41: 
   42:   const parsed = parseTranscript(client, source)
   43:   if (!transcriptHasContent(parsed)) {
   44:     process.stderr.write(`source transcript does not contain any importable messages or tool events: ${source}\n`)
   45:     return 1
   46:   }
   47: 
   48:   const detectedProject = inferProjectSlug(parsed)
   49:   const matchesCurrentRepo = options.forceRepo || transcriptMatchesRepo(parsed, root, repoSlug)
   50: 
   51:   const result = await importTranscript(
   52:     parsed,
   53:     root,
   54:     globalRoot,
   55:     matchesCurrentRepo ? repoSlug : detectedProject,
   56:     matchesCurrentRepo ? options.rawUploadPermission : 'not-applicable',
   57:     matchesCurrentRepo,
   58:   )
   59: 
   60:   const output = {
   61:     ...result,
   62:     matches_current_repo: matchesCurrentRepo,
   63:     detected_project: detectedProject,
   64:   } as Record<string, unknown>
   65: 
   66:   if (options.format === 'json') {
   67:     process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(output) + '\n')
   68:   } else {
   69:     process.stdout.write(formatImportText(output) + '\n')
   70:   }
   71:   return 0
   72: }
   73: 
   74: function formatImportText(result: Record<string, unknown>): string {
   75:   return [
   76:     `client: ${result['client'] ?? ''}`,
   77:     `project: ${result['project'] ?? ''}`,
   78:     `session_id: ${result['session_id'] ?? ''}`,
   79:     `started_at: ${result['started_at'] ?? ''}`,
   80:     `detected_project: ${result['detected_project'] ?? ''}`,
   81:     `matches_current_repo: ${String(result['matches_current_repo'] ?? false).toLowerCase()}`,
   82:     `raw_upload_permission: ${result['raw_upload_permission'] ?? ''}`,
   83:     `message_count: ${result['message_count'] ?? 0}`,
   84:     `tool_event_count: ${result['tool_event_count'] ?? 0}`,
   85:     `repo_raw_path: ${result['repo_raw_path'] ?? ''}`,
   86:     `repo_clean_path: ${result['repo_clean_path'] ?? ''}`,
   87:     `repo_manifest_path: ${result['repo_manifest_path'] ?? ''}`,
   88:     `global_raw_path: ${result['global_raw_path'] ?? ''}`,
   89:     `global_clean_path: ${result['global_clean_path'] ?? ''}`,
   90:   ].join('\n')
   91: }

    1: import { resolve } from 'node:path'
    2: 
    3: import { findProjectForPath, loadConfig, type ProjectEntry } from '../heartbeat/config.js'
    4: import {
    5:   disableManagedCaddy,
    6:   enableManagedCaddy,
    7:   isCaddyInstalled,
    8:   loadManagedCaddyStatus,
    9:   managedCaddyStartCommand,
   10: } from '../caddy/manager.js'
   11: 
   12: export async function cmdCaddyEnable(options: { root: string }): Promise<number> {
   13:   const project = resolveProject(options.root)
   14:   if (project === null) {
   15:     process.stderr.write(
   16:       'Project not found in ~/.memorytree/config.toml. Run `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` first.\n',
   17:     )
   18:     return 1
   19:   }
   20: 
   21:   if (!isCaddyInstalled()) {
   22:     process.stderr.write('Caddy is not installed or not available on PATH. Install Caddy first.\n')
   23:     return 1
   24:   }
   25: 
   26:   try {
   27:     const result = enableManagedCaddy(project)
   28:     process.stdout.write(`Caddy enabled for project: ${project.name}\n`)
   29:     process.stdout.write(`Port: ${String(project.report_port)}\n`)
   30:     process.stdout.write(`Exposure: ${project.report_exposure}\n`)
   31:     process.stdout.write(`Report directory: ${result.reportDir}\n`)
   32:     process.stdout.write(`Main config: ${result.mainConfigPath}\n`)
   33:     process.stdout.write(`Project fragment: ${result.fragmentPath}\n`)
   34:     for (const url of result.localUrls) {
   35:       process.stdout.write(`Local URL: ${url}\n`)
   36:     }
   37:     for (const url of result.lanUrls) {
   38:       process.stdout.write(`LAN URL: ${url}\n`)
   39:     }
   40:     return 0
   41:   } catch (error: unknown) {
   42:     process.stderr.write(`${formatError(error)}\n`)
   43:     return 1
   44:   }
   45: }
   46: 
   47: export async function cmdCaddyDisable(options: { root: string }): Promise<number> {
   48:   const project = resolveProject(options.root)
   49:   if (project === null) {
   50:     process.stderr.write(
   51:       'Project not found in ~/.memorytree/config.toml. Run `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` first.\n',
   52:     )
   53:     return 1
   54:   }
   55: 
   56:   if (!isCaddyInstalled()) {
   57:     process.stderr.write('Caddy is not installed or not available on PATH. Install Caddy first.\n')
   58:     return 1
   59:   }
   60: 
   61:   try {
   62:     const result = disableManagedCaddy(project)
   63:     process.stdout.write(`Caddy disabled for project: ${project.name}\n`)
   64:     process.stdout.write(`Main config: ${result.mainConfigPath}\n`)
   65:     process.stdout.write(`Project fragment removed: ${result.fragmentPath}\n`)
   66:     return 0
   67:   } catch (error: unknown) {
   68:     process.stderr.write(`${formatError(error)}\n`)
   69:     return 1
   70:   }
   71: }
   72: 
   73: export async function cmdCaddyStatus(options: { root: string }): Promise<number> {
   74:   const project = resolveProject(options.root)
   75:   if (project === null) {
   76:     process.stderr.write(
   77:       'Project not found in ~/.memorytree/config.toml. Run `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` first.\n',
   78:     )
   79:     return 1
   80:   }
   81: 
   82:   const status = await loadManagedCaddyStatus(project)
   83:   process.stdout.write(`Caddy installed: ${yesNo(status.installed)}\n`)
   84:   process.stdout.write(`Caddy running: ${yesNo(status.running)}\n`)
   85:   process.stdout.write(`Using MemoryTree Caddyfile: ${yesNoOrUnknown(status.usingManagedConfig)}\n`)
   86:   process.stdout.write(`Project registered: ${yesNo(status.fragmentExists)}\n`)
   87:   process.stdout.write(`Port: ${String(status.port)}\n`)
   88:   process.stdout.write(`Exposure: ${status.exposure}\n`)
   89:   process.stdout.write(`Report directory: ${status.reportDir}\n`)
   90:   process.stdout.write(`Main config: ${status.mainConfigPath}\n`)
   91:   process.stdout.write(`Project fragment: ${status.fragmentPath}\n`)
   92:   for (const url of status.localUrls) {
   93:     process.stdout.write(`Local URL: ${url}\n`)
   94:   }
   95:   for (const url of status.lanUrls) {
   96:     process.stdout.write(`LAN URL: ${url}\n`)
   97:   }
   98:   if (!status.installed || !status.running || status.usingManagedConfig !== true) {
   99:     process.stdout.write(`Managed start command: ${managedCaddyStartCommand()}\n`)
  100:   }
  101:   return 0
  102: }
  103: 
  104: function resolveProject(root: string): ProjectEntry | null {
  105:   const config = loadConfig()
  106:   return findProjectForPath(config, resolve(root))
  107: }
  108: 
  109: function yesNo(value: boolean): string {
  110:   return value ? 'yes' : 'no'
  111: }
  112: 
  113: function yesNoOrUnknown(value: boolean | null): string {
  114:   if (value === null) return 'unknown'
  115:   return yesNo(value)
  116: }
  117: 
  118: function formatError(error: unknown): string {
  119:   if (error instanceof Error && error.message) {
  120:     return error.message
  121:   }
  122:   return String(error)
  123: }

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
    1: /**
    2:  * Recall 鈥?on-demand transcript sync + latest session lookup.
    3:  * Port of scripts/recall-session.py
    4:  */
    5: 
    6: import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    7: import { basename, resolve, join } from 'node:path'
    8: 
    9: import initSqlJs from 'sql.js'
   10: 
   11: import { slugify } from '../transcript/common.js'
   12: import { defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot, discoverSourceFiles, transcriptMatchesRepo } from '../transcript/discover.js'
   13: import { importTranscript, transcriptHasContent } from '../transcript/import.js'
   14: import { parseTranscript } from '../transcript/parse.js'
   15: import type { ParsedTranscript, TranscriptEvent } from '../types/transcript.js'
   16: import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
   17: 
   18: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   19: // Types
   20: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   21: 
   22: export interface RecallResult {
   23:   found: boolean
   24:   project: string
   25:   repo: string
   26:   imported_count: number
   27:   message?: string
   28:   client?: string
   29:   session_id?: string
   30:   title?: string
   31:   started_at?: string
   32:   cwd?: string
   33:   branch?: string
   34:   message_count?: number
   35:   tool_event_count?: number
   36:   event_count?: number
   37:   global_clean_path?: string
   38:   global_full_path?: string
   39:   clean_content?: string
   40:   normalized_content?: string
   41: }
   42: 
   43: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   44: // Public API
   45: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   46: 
   47: export async function recall(
   48:   root: string,
   49:   projectName: string,
   50:   globalRootOverride: string,
   51:   activationTime: string,
   52: ): Promise<RecallResult> {
   53:   const resolvedRoot = resolve(root)
   54:   const repoSlug = slugify(projectName.trim() || basename(resolvedRoot), 'project')
   55:   const globalRoot = globalRootOverride ? resolve(globalRootOverride) : defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot()
   56:   const effectiveActivation = activationTime || nowIso()
   57: 
   58:   const imported = await syncCurrentProject(resolvedRoot, repoSlug, globalRoot)
   59: 
   60:   const session = await findLatestSession(globalRoot, resolvedRoot, repoSlug, effectiveActivation)
   61: 
   62:   if (session === null) {
   63:     return {
   64:       found: false,
   65:       project: repoSlug,
   66:       repo: toPosixPath(resolvedRoot),
   67:       imported_count: imported,
   68:       message: 'No previous session found for this project.',
   69:     }
   70:   }
   71: 
   72:   const cleanPath = String(session['global_clean_path'] ?? '')
   73:   const fullPath = String(session['global_full_path'] ?? '')
   74:   let cleanContent = ''
   75:   if (cleanPath && existsSync(cleanPath)) {
   76:     try {
   77:       cleanContent = readFileSync(cleanPath, 'utf-8')
   78:     } catch {
   79:       // ignore read errors
   80:     }
   81:   }
   82:   const normalizedContent = loadNormalizedContent(fullPath)
   83: 
   84:   return {
   85:     found: true,
   86:     project: repoSlug,
   87:     repo: toPosixPath(resolvedRoot),
   88:     imported_count: imported,
   89:     client: String(session['client'] ?? ''),
   90:     session_id: String(session['session_id'] ?? ''),
   91:     title: String(session['title'] ?? ''),
   92:     started_at: String(session['started_at'] ?? ''),
   93:     cwd: String(session['cwd'] ?? ''),
   94:     branch: String(session['branch'] ?? ''),
   95:     message_count: Number(session['message_count'] ?? 0),
   96:     tool_event_count: Number(session['tool_event_count'] ?? 0),
   97:     event_count: Number(session['event_count'] ?? 0),
   98:     global_clean_path: cleanPath,
   99:     global_full_path: fullPath,
  100:     clean_content: cleanContent,
  101:     normalized_content: normalizedContent,
  102:   }
  103: }
  104: 
  105: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  106: // Sync current project
  107: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  108: 
  109: export async function syncCurrentProject(root: string, repoSlug: string, globalRoot: string): Promise<number> {
  110:   const discovered = discoverSourceFiles()
  111:   let imported = 0
  112: 
  113:   for (const [client, source] of discovered) {
  114:     let parsed
  115:     try {
  116:       parsed = parseTranscript(client, source)
  117:     } catch {
  118:       continue
  119:     }
  120: 
  121:     if (!transcriptHasContent(parsed)) continue
  122:     if (!transcriptMatchesRepo(parsed, root, repoSlug)) continue
  123: 
  124:     try {
  125:       await importTranscript(parsed, root, globalRoot, repoSlug, 'not-set', true)
  126:       imported++
  127:     } catch {
  128:       continue
  129:     }
  130:   }
  131: 
  132:   return imported
  133: }
  134: 
  135: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  136: // Find latest session
  137: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  138: 
  139: export async function findLatestSession(
  140:   globalRoot: string,
  141:   root: string,
  142:   repoSlug: string,
  143:   activationTime: string,
  144: ): Promise<Record<string, unknown> | null> {
  145:   const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')
  146:   if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
  147:     return findLatestFromJsonl(globalRoot, root, repoSlug, activationTime)
  148:   }
  149: 
  150:   try {
  151:     const SQL = await initSqlJs()
  152:     const db = new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))
  153:     try {
  154:       const stmt = db.prepare(
  155:         'SELECT * FROM transcripts WHERE started_at < ? ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 20',
  156:       )
  157:       stmt.bind([activationTime])
  158: 
  159:       const resolvedRoot = toPosixPath(resolve(root)).toLowerCase()
  160:       const columns = stmt.getColumnNames()
  161: 
  162:       while (stmt.step()) {
  163:         const values = stmt.get()
  164:         const row: Record<string, unknown> = {}
  165:         for (let i = 0; i < columns.length; i++) {
  166:           row[columns[i]!] = values[i]
  167:         }
  168:         const cwd = String(row['cwd'] ?? '')
  169:         const project = String(row['project'] ?? '')
  170:         if (cwdMatches(cwd, resolvedRoot) || project === repoSlug) {
  171:           stmt.free()
  172:           return row
  173:         }
  174:       }
  175:       stmt.free()
  176:     } finally {
  177:       db.close()
  178:     }
  179:   } catch {
  180:     return findLatestFromJsonl(globalRoot, root, repoSlug, activationTime)
  181:   }
  182: 
  183:   return null
  184: }
  185: 
  186: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  187: // JSONL fallback
  188: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  189: 
  190: export function findLatestFromJsonl(
  191:   globalRoot: string,
  192:   root: string,
  193:   repoSlug: string,
  194:   activationTime: string,
  195: ): Record<string, unknown> | null {
  196:   const jsonlPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')
  197:   if (!existsSync(jsonlPath)) return null
  198: 
  199:   const resolvedRoot = toPosixPath(resolve(root)).toLowerCase()
  200:   const candidates: Record<string, unknown>[] = []
  201: 
  202:   let content: string
  203:   try {
  204:     content = readFileSync(jsonlPath, 'utf-8')
  205:   } catch {
  206:     return null
  207:   }
  208: 
  209:   for (const line of content.split('\n')) {
  210:     const trimmed = line.trim()
  211:     if (!trimmed) continue
  212:     let entry: Record<string, unknown>
  213:     try {
  214:       entry = JSON.parse(trimmed) as Record<string, unknown>
  215:     } catch {
  216:       continue
  217:     }
  218: 
  219:     const started = String(entry['started_at'] ?? '')
  220:     if (started >= activationTime) continue
  221: 
  222:     const cwd = String(entry['cwd'] ?? '')
  223:     const project = String(entry['project'] ?? '')
  224:     if (cwdMatches(cwd, resolvedRoot) || project === repoSlug) {
  225:       candidates.push(entry)
  226:     }
  227:   }
  228: 
  229:   if (candidates.length === 0) return null
  230: 
  231:   candidates.sort((a, b) => {
  232:     const aTime = String(a['started_at'] ?? '')
  233:     const bTime = String(b['started_at'] ?? '')
  234:     return bTime.localeCompare(aTime)
  235:   })
  236:   return candidates[0]!
  237: }
  238: 
  239: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  240: // CWD matching
  241: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  242: 
  243: export function cwdMatches(cwd: string, resolvedRoot: string): boolean {
  244:   if (!cwd) return false
  245:   try {
  246:     const cwdResolved = toPosixPath(resolve(cwd)).toLowerCase()
  247:     if (cwdResolved === resolvedRoot) return true
  248:     const prefix = resolvedRoot.endsWith('/') ? resolvedRoot : resolvedRoot + '/'
  249:     return cwdResolved.startsWith(prefix)
  250:   } catch {
  251:     return false
  252:   }
  253: }
  254: 
  255: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  256: // Helpers
  257: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  258: 
  259: function nowIso(): string {
  260:   return new Date().toISOString().replace(/\.\d{3}Z$/, 'Z')
  261: }
  262: 
  263: export function formatText(payload: RecallResult): string {
  264:   if (!payload.found) {
  265:     return [
  266:       `project: ${payload.project}`,
  267:       `imported: ${payload.imported_count}`,
  268:       `result: ${payload.message ?? 'No previous session found.'}`,
  269:     ].join('\n')
  270:   }
  271: 
  272:   const lines = [
  273:     `project: ${payload.project}`,
  274:     `client: ${payload.client ?? ''}`,
  275:     `session_id: ${payload.session_id ?? ''}`,
  276:     `title: ${payload.title ?? ''}`,
  277:     `started_at: ${payload.started_at ?? ''}`,
  278:     `cwd: ${payload.cwd ?? ''}`,
  279:     `branch: ${payload.branch ?? ''}`,
  280:     `messages: ${payload.message_count ?? 0}`,
  281:     `tool_events: ${payload.tool_event_count ?? 0}`,
  282:     `events: ${payload.event_count ?? 0}`,
  283:     `imported_this_sync: ${payload.imported_count}`,
  284:     `clean_transcript: ${payload.global_clean_path ?? ''}`,
  285:   ]
  286: 
  287:   if (payload.global_full_path) {
  288:     lines.push(`full_transcript: ${payload.global_full_path}`)
  289:   }
  290: 
  291:   if (payload.normalized_content) {
  292:     lines.push('', '--- normalized transcript content ---', payload.normalized_content)
  293:   } else if (payload.clean_content) {
  294:     lines.push('', '--- clean transcript content ---', payload.clean_content)
  295:   }
  296: 
  297:   return lines.join('\n')
  298: }
  299: 
  300: function loadNormalizedContent(fullPath: string): string {
  301:   if (!fullPath || !existsSync(fullPath)) return ''
  302: 
  303:   try {
  304:     const raw = readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8')
  305:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as ParsedTranscript
  306:     if (Array.isArray(parsed.events) && parsed.events.length > 0) {
  307:       return formatNormalizedEvents(parsed.events)
  308:     }
  309:     if (Array.isArray(parsed.messages) && parsed.messages.length > 0) {
  310:       return parsed.messages
  311:         .map(message => {
  312:           const time = message.timestamp?.slice(11, 19) || '--:--:--'
  313:           return `[${time}] ${message.role}: ${message.text}`
  314:         })
  315:         .join('\n')
  316:     }
  317:   } catch {
  318:     return ''
  319:   }
  320: 
  321:   return ''
  322: }
  323: 
  324: function formatNormalizedEvents(events: TranscriptEvent[]): string {
  325:   return events
  326:     .map(event => {
  327:       const time = event.timestamp?.slice(11, 19) || '--:--:--'
  328:       if (event.kind === 'message') {
  329:         return `[${time}] ${event.role}: ${event.text}`
  330:       }
  331:       if (event.kind === 'reasoning') {
  332:         return `[${time}] reasoning: ${event.summary || event.text || 'Reasoning step recorded.'}`
  333:       }
  334:       if (event.kind === 'tool_call') {
  335:         return `[${time}] tool_call ${event.tool_name}${event.call_id ? ` (${event.call_id})` : ''}: ${event.summary || event.tool_name}`
  336:       }
  337:       if (event.kind === 'tool_result') {
  338:         const suffix = typeof event.exit_code === 'number' ? ` exit=${event.exit_code}` : ''
  339:         return `[${time}] tool_result ${event.tool_name}${event.call_id ? ` (${event.call_id})` : ''}${suffix}: ${event.summary || `${event.tool_name} result`}`
  340:       }
  341:       if (event.kind === 'task_status') {
  342:         return `[${time}] task_${event.status}: ${event.summary || `Task ${event.status}`}`
  343:       }
  344:       if (event.kind === 'token_count') {
  345:         return `[${time}] token_count: ${event.summary || 'Token usage updated.'}`
  346:       }
  347:       return `[${time}] ${event.title || event.kind}: ${event.summary || ''}`.trim()
  348:     })
  349:     .join('\n')
  350: }

    4: 
    5: import { memorytreeRoot, type ProjectEntry } from '../heartbeat/config.js'
    6: import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
    7: import { execCommand } from '../utils/exec.js'
    8: 
    9: const CADDY_ADMIN_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:2019'
   10: const CADDY_ADMIN_ORIGIN = 'http://127.0.0.1:2019'
   11: 
   12: export interface ManagedCaddyPaths {
   13:   readonly rootDir: string
   14:   readonly sitesDir: string
   15:   readonly mainConfigPath: string
   16:   readonly fragmentPath: string
   17: }
   18: 
   19: export interface ManagedCaddyStatus {
   20:   readonly installed: boolean
   21:   readonly running: boolean
   22:   readonly usingManagedConfig: boolean | null
   23:   readonly mainConfigPath: string
   24:   readonly mainConfigExists: boolean
   25:   readonly fragmentPath: string
   26:   readonly fragmentExists: boolean
   27:   readonly port: number
   28:   readonly exposure: ProjectEntry['report_exposure']
   29:   readonly reportDir: string
   30:   readonly localUrls: readonly string[]
   31:   readonly lanUrls: readonly string[]
   32: }
   33: 
   34: export interface ManagedCaddyEnableResult {
   35:   readonly mainConfigPath: string
   36:   readonly fragmentPath: string
   37:   readonly reportDir: string
   38:   readonly localUrls: readonly string[]
   39:   readonly lanUrls: readonly string[]
   40: }
   41: 
   42: export function managedCaddyPaths(projectId: string): ManagedCaddyPaths {
   43:   const rootDir = resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'caddy')
   44:   const sitesDir = resolve(rootDir, 'sites')
   45:   return {
   46:     rootDir,
   47:     sitesDir,
   48:     mainConfigPath: resolve(rootDir, 'Caddyfile'),
   49:     fragmentPath: resolve(sitesDir, `${projectId}.caddy`),
   50:   }
   51: }
   52: 
   53: export function projectReportDir(project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'development_path'>): string {
   54:   return resolve(project.development_path, 'Memory', '07_reports')
   55: }
   56: 
   57: export function renderManagedMainCaddyfile(): string {
   58:   return [
   59:     '# Managed by MemoryTree. Do not edit by hand.',
   60:     '{',
   61:     '  auto_https off',
   62:     '}',
   63:     '',
   64:     'import sites/*.caddy',
   65:     '',
   66:   ].join('\n')
   67: }
   68: 
   69: export function renderManagedProjectFragment(
   70:   project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'id' | 'name' | 'report_port' | 'report_exposure'>,
   71:   reportDir: string,
   72: ): string {
   73:   const lines = [
   74:     '# Managed by MemoryTree. Do not edit by hand.',
   75:     `# project_id: ${project.id}`,
   76:     `# project_name: ${project.name}`,
   77:     `# report_port: ${String(project.report_port)}`,
   78:     `# report_exposure: ${project.report_exposure}`,
   79:     '',
   80:     managedSiteAddress(project.report_port, project.report_exposure),
   81:     '{',
   82:   ]
   83: 
   84:   if (project.report_exposure === 'local') {
   85:     lines.push('  bind 127.0.0.1 [::1]')
   86:   }
   87: 
   88:   lines.push(`  root * ${quoteForCaddyPath(reportDir)}`)
   89:   lines.push('  file_server')
   90:   lines.push('}')
   91:   lines.push('')
   92:   return lines.join('\n')
   93: }
   94: 
   95: export function isCaddyInstalled(): boolean {
   96:   try {
   97:     execCommand('caddy', ['version'])
   98:     return true
   99:   } catch {
  100:     return false
  101:   }
  102: }
  103: 
  104: export function findManagedPortConflict(projectId: string, reportPort: number): string | null {
  105:   const { sitesDir, fragmentPath } = managedCaddyPaths(projectId)
  106:   if (!existsSync(sitesDir)) return null
  107: 
  108:   for (const entry of readdirSync(sitesDir)) {
  109:     if (!entry.endsWith('.caddy')) continue
  110:     const currentPath = resolve(sitesDir, entry)
  111:     if (currentPath === fragmentPath) continue
  112: 
  113:     const text = readFileSync(currentPath, 'utf-8')
  114:     const portMatch = text.match(/^# report_port:\s*(\d+)\s*$/m)
  115:     if (!portMatch) continue
  116: 
  117:     const currentPort = parseInt(portMatch[1]!, 10)
  118:     if (currentPort !== reportPort) continue
  119: 
  120:     const projectMatch = text.match(/^# project_id:\s*(.+)\s*$/m)
  121:     return projectMatch?.[1]?.trim() || entry.replace(/\.caddy$/i, '')
  122:   }
  123: 
  124:   return null
  125: }
  126: 
  127: export function enableManagedCaddy(project: ProjectEntry): ManagedCaddyEnableResult {
  128:   const paths = managedCaddyPaths(project.id)
  129:   const conflictProjectId = findManagedPortConflict(project.id, project.report_port)
  130:   if (conflictProjectId !== null) {
  131:     throw new Error(
  132:       `Port ${String(project.report_port)} is already used by another managed Caddy site (${conflictProjectId}). ` +
  133:       'Change this project\'s report_port first, then rerun the command.',
  134:     )
  135:   }
  136: 
  137:   mkdirSync(paths.rootDir, { recursive: true })
  138:   mkdirSync(paths.sitesDir, { recursive: true })
  139: 
  140:   const reportDir = projectReportDir(project)
  141:   mkdirSync(reportDir, { recursive: true })
  142: 
  143:   writeTextIfChanged(paths.mainConfigPath, renderManagedMainCaddyfile())
  144:   writeTextIfChanged(paths.fragmentPath, renderManagedProjectFragment(project, reportDir))
  145:   validateManagedCaddyConfig(paths.mainConfigPath)
  146:   try {
  147:     reloadManagedCaddy(paths.mainConfigPath)
  148:   } catch (error: unknown) {
  149:     throw new Error(
  150:       `Caddy config files were updated, but reload failed. ${formatExecError(error)} ` +
  151:       `Start Caddy with: ${managedCaddyStartCommand()}`,
  152:     )
  153:   }
  154: 
  155:   return {
  156:     mainConfigPath: paths.mainConfigPath,
  157:     fragmentPath: paths.fragmentPath,
  158:     reportDir,
  159:     localUrls: localUrlsForProject(project.report_port),
  160:     lanUrls: lanUrlsForProject(project.report_port, project.report_exposure),
  161:   }
  162: }
  163: 
  164: export function disableManagedCaddy(project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'id' | 'report_port' | 'report_exposure'>): ManagedCaddyEnableResult {
  165:   const paths = managedCaddyPaths(project.id)
  166:   if (existsSync(paths.fragmentPath)) {
  167:     rmSync(paths.fragmentPath, { force: true })
  168:   }
  169: 
  170:   if (existsSync(paths.mainConfigPath)) {
  171:     validateManagedCaddyConfig(paths.mainConfigPath)
  172:     try {
  173:       reloadManagedCaddy(paths.mainConfigPath)
  174:     } catch (error: unknown) {
  175:       throw new Error(
  176:         `The project fragment was removed, but Caddy reload failed. ${formatExecError(error)} ` +
  177:         `Start Caddy with: ${managedCaddyStartCommand()}`,
  178:       )
  179:     }
  180:   }
  181: 
  182:   return {
  183:     mainConfigPath: paths.mainConfigPath,
  184:     fragmentPath: paths.fragmentPath,
  185:     reportDir: '',
  186:     localUrls: localUrlsForProject(project.report_port),
  187:     lanUrls: lanUrlsForProject(project.report_port, project.report_exposure),
  188:   }
  189: }
  190: 
  191: export async function loadManagedCaddyStatus(project: ProjectEntry): Promise<ManagedCaddyStatus> {
  192:   const paths = managedCaddyPaths(project.id)
  193:   const installed = isCaddyInstalled()
  194:   const mainConfigExists = existsSync(paths.mainConfigPath)
  195:   const fragmentExists = existsSync(paths.fragmentPath)
  196:   const runningConfigText = installed ? await fetchRunningCaddyConfigText() : null
  197:   const running = runningConfigText !== null
  198: 
  199:   let usingManagedConfig: boolean | null = null
  200:   if (installed && running && mainConfigExists) {
  201:     const managedConfigText = adaptManagedCaddyConfig(paths.mainConfigPath)
  202:     usingManagedConfig = stableJsonFromText(managedConfigText) === stableJsonFromText(runningConfigText)
  203:   }
  204: 
  205:   return {
  206:     installed,
  207:     running,
  208:     usingManagedConfig,
  209:     mainConfigPath: paths.mainConfigPath,
  210:     mainConfigExists,
  211:     fragmentPath: paths.fragmentPath,
  212:     fragmentExists,
  213:     port: project.report_port,
  214:     exposure: project.report_exposure,
  215:     reportDir: projectReportDir(project),
  216:     localUrls: localUrlsForProject(project.report_port),
  217:     lanUrls: lanUrlsForProject(project.report_port, project.report_exposure),
  218:   }
  219: }
  220: 
  221: export function managedCaddyStartCommand(): string {
  222:   const mainConfigPath = managedCaddyPaths('placeholder').mainConfigPath
  223:   return `caddy run --config "${mainConfigPath}" --adapter caddyfile`
  224: }
  225: 
  226: function managedSiteAddress(reportPort: number, exposure: ProjectEntry['report_exposure']): string {
  227:   if (exposure === 'lan') {
  228:     return `http://:${String(reportPort)}`
  229:   }
  230:   return `http://127.0.0.1:${String(reportPort)}, http://localhost:${String(reportPort)}`
  231: }
  232: 
  233: function quoteForCaddyPath(value: string): string {
  234:   const normalized = toPosixPath(resolve(value)).replace(/"/g, '\\"')
  235:   return `"${normalized}"`
  236: }
  237: 
  238: function writeTextIfChanged(path: string, content: string): void {
  239:   if (existsSync(path) && readFileSync(path, 'utf-8') === content) {
  240:     return
  241:   }
  242:   writeFileSync(path, content, 'utf-8')
  243: }
  244: 
  245: function validateManagedCaddyConfig(mainConfigPath: string): void {
  246:   execCommand('caddy', ['adapt', '--config', mainConfigPath, '--adapter', 'caddyfile', '--validate'], {
  247:     allowFailure: false,
  248:   })
  249: }
  250: 
  251: function reloadManagedCaddy(mainConfigPath: string): void {
  252:   execCommand('caddy', ['reload', '--config', mainConfigPath, '--adapter', 'caddyfile'])
  253: }
  254: 
  255: function adaptManagedCaddyConfig(mainConfigPath: string): string {
  256:   return execCommand('caddy', ['adapt', '--config', mainConfigPath, '--adapter', 'caddyfile'])
  257: }
  258: 
  259: async function fetchRunningCaddyConfigText(): Promise<string | null> {
  260:   try {
  261:     const response = await fetch(`${CADDY_ADMIN_URL}/config/`, {
  262:       headers: {
  263:         Origin: CADDY_ADMIN_ORIGIN,
  264:       },
  265:     })
  266:     if (!response.ok) return null
  267:     return await response.text()
  268:   } catch {
  269:     return null
  270:   }
  271: }
  272: 
  273: function stableJsonFromText(text: string | null): string | null {
  274:   if (text === null) return null
  275:   try {
  276:     return JSON.stringify(sortJsonKeys(JSON.parse(text)))
  277:   } catch {
  278:     return null
  279:   }
  280: }
  281: 
  282: function sortJsonKeys(value: unknown): unknown {
  283:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
  284:     return value.map(item => sortJsonKeys(item))
  285:   }
  286:   if (value !== null && typeof value === 'object') {
  287:     return Object.fromEntries(
  288:       Object.keys(value)
  289:         .sort((left, right) => left.localeCompare(right))
  290:         .map(key => [key, sortJsonKeys((value as Record<string, unknown>)[key])]),
  291:     )
  292:   }
  293:   return value
  294: }
  295: 
  296: function localUrlsForProject(reportPort: number): string[] {
  297:   return [
  298:     `http://127.0.0.1:${String(reportPort)}/`,
  299:     `http://localhost:${String(reportPort)}/`,
  300:   ]
  301: }
  302: 
  303: function lanUrlsForProject(
  304:   reportPort: number,
  305:   exposure: ProjectEntry['report_exposure'],
  306: ): string[] {
  307:   if (exposure !== 'lan') return []
  308: 
  309:   const urls = new Set<string>()
  310:   const interfaces = networkInterfaces()
  311:   for (const addresses of Object.values(interfaces)) {
  312:     for (const address of addresses ?? []) {
  313:       if (address.family !== 'IPv4' || address.internal) continue
  314:       urls.add(`http://${address.address}:${String(reportPort)}/`)
  315:     }
  316:   }
  317:   return [...urls].sort((left, right) => left.localeCompare(right))
  318: }
  319: 
  320: function formatExecError(error: unknown): string {
  321:   if (error instanceof Error && error.message) {
  322:     return error.message
  323:   }
  324:   return String(error)
  325: }

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
    1: /**
    2:  * Subprocess execution wrapper for git and other commands.
    3:  * Port of heartbeat.py _git() and daemon subprocess calls.
    4:  */
    5: 
    6: import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process'
    7: 
    8: export function execCommand(
    9:   command: string,
   10:   args: readonly string[],
   11:   options?: { cwd?: string; timeout?: number; allowFailure?: boolean },
   12: ): string {
   13:   const cwd = options?.cwd
   14:   const timeout = options?.timeout ?? 120_000
   15:   const allowFailure = options?.allowFailure ?? false
   16: 
   17:   try {
   18:     const stdout = execFileSync(command, [...args], {
   19:       cwd,
   20:       timeout,
   21:       encoding: 'utf-8',
   22:       stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
   23:     })
   24:     return stdout
   25:   } catch (err: unknown) {
   26:     if (allowFailure) {
   27:       const e = err as { stdout?: string }
   28:       return typeof e.stdout === 'string' ? e.stdout : ''
   29:     }
   30:     throw err
   31:   }
   32: }
   33: 
   34: export function git(cwd: string, ...args: string[]): string {
   35:   const allowFailure = args[0] === 'status' || args[0] === 'remote'
   36:   return execCommand('git', args, { cwd, allowFailure })
   37: }

Using Node v20.20.1
    1: import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    2: import { dirname, join, normalize, resolve } from 'node:path'
    3: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
    4: 
    5: /**
    6:  * Convert a file path to posix format (forward slashes).
    7:  * All paths written to files (JSON / SQLite / Markdown / JSONL) must use posix format.
    8:  */
    9: export function toPosixPath(p: string): string {
   10:   return normalize(p.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/+/g, '/')).replace(/\\/g, '/')
   11: }
   12: 
   13: /**
   14:  * Resolve the installed skill root from a module URL.
   15:  * Works for both source execution (`src/**`) and built execution (`dist/**`).
   16:  */
   17: export function resolveSkillRoot(moduleUrl: string): string {
   18:   let current = dirname(fileURLToPath(moduleUrl))
   19: 
   20:   while (current) {
   21:     if (existsSync(join(current, 'SKILL.md')) && existsSync(join(current, 'assets', 'templates'))) {
   22:       return current
   23:     }
   24: 
   25:     const parent = resolve(current, '..')
   26:     if (parent === current) {
   27:       break
   28:     }
   29:     current = parent
   30:   }
   31: 
   32:   throw new Error(`Unable to resolve skill root from ${moduleUrl}`)
   33: }

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
    1: /**
    2:  * Collect transcript manifest directories from other registered projects so a
    3:  * report can include cross-project sessions in one place.
    4:  */
    5: 
    6: import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    7: import { join, resolve } from 'node:path'
    8: 
    9: import { findProjectForPath, loadConfig, projectExecutionPath } from '../heartbeat/config.js'
   10: 
   11: export function collectExtraManifestDirs(root: string): string[] {
   12:   const resolvedRoot = resolve(root)
   13:   const config = loadConfig()
   14:   const currentProject = findProjectForPath(config, resolvedRoot)
   15:   if (currentProject === null) {
   16:     return []
   17:   }
   18: 
   19:   const seen = new Set<string>()
   20:   const dirs: string[] = []
   21: 
   22:   for (const project of config.projects) {
   23:     if (project.id === currentProject.id) continue
   24: 
   25:     const manifestsDir = resolve(join(projectExecutionPath(project), 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'manifests'))
   26:     if (!existsSync(manifestsDir) || seen.has(manifestsDir)) continue
   27: 
   28:     seen.add(manifestsDir)
   29:     dirs.push(manifestsDir)
   30:   }
   31: 
   32:   return dirs

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
    1: import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
    2: import { mkdtempSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
    3: import { join } from 'node:path'
    4: import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
    5: 
    6: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    7: // Mock homedir so any internal path helpers (logging, alerts, config, lock)
    8: // all point to a temp directory and don't pollute the real home.
    9: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   10: 
   11: let tmpDir: string
   12: 
   13: vi.mock('node:os', async () => {
   14:   const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('node:os')>('node:os')
   15:   return {
   16:     ...actual,
   17:     homedir: () => tmpDir,
   18:   }
   19: })
   20: 
   21: import { scanSensitive } from '../../src/heartbeat/heartbeat.js'
   22: import { _resetLogger, setupLogging } from '../../src/heartbeat/log.js'
   23: import { readAlerts, clearAlerts } from '../../src/heartbeat/alert.js'
   24: import type { ParsedTranscript } from '../../src/types/transcript.js'
   25: 
   26: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   27: // Setup / Teardown
   28: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   29: 
   30: beforeEach(() => {
   31:   tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'hb-test-'))
   32:   _resetLogger()
   33:   setupLogging('warn') // suppress info/debug noise during tests
   34: })
   35: 
   36: afterEach(() => {
   37:   clearAlerts()
   38:   _resetLogger()
   39:   rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
   40: })
   41: 
   42: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   43: // Helpers
   44: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   45: 
   46: function makeTranscript(texts: string[]): ParsedTranscript {
   47:   return {
   48:     client: 'claude',
   49:     session_id: 'test-session',
   50:     title: 'Test',
   51:     started_at: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z',
   52:     cwd: '/tmp/project',
   53:     branch: 'main',
   54:     messages: texts.map(text => ({ role: 'user', text, timestamp: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' })),
   55:     tool_events: [],
   56:     source_path: '/tmp/transcript.json',
   57:   }
   58: }
   59: 
   60: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   61: // scanSensitive 鈥?detection
   62: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   63: 
   64: describe('scanSensitive', () => {
   65:   it('detects API key pattern: api_key = sk-1234567890', () => {
   66:     const parsed = makeTranscript(['Set api_key = sk-1234567890abcdef'])
   67:     scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   68: 
   69:     const alerts = readAlerts()
   70:     expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   71:     expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
   72:   })
   73: 
   74:   it('detects password pattern: password = secret123', () => {
   75:     const parsed = makeTranscript(['password = secret123'])
   76:     scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   77: 
   78:     const alerts = readAlerts()
   79:     expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   80:     expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
   81:   })
   82: 
   83:   it('detects token pattern: token = abc123xyz', () => {
   84:     const parsed = makeTranscript(['secret = my-secret-value'])
   85:     scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   86: 
   87:     const alerts = readAlerts()
   88:     expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   89:     expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
   90:   })
   91: 
   92:   it('detects known secret prefixes: sk-xxxx, ghp_xxxx', () => {
   93:     const parsed = makeTranscript(['Use sk-abcdef1234567890extra'])
   94:     scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
   95: 
   96:     const alerts = readAlerts()
   97:     expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
   98:     expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
   99:   })
  100: 
  101:   it('detects GitHub PAT prefix: ghp_xxxx', () => {
  102:     clearAlerts()
  103:     const parsed = makeTranscript(['Token ghp_abcdef1234567890extraextra'])
  104:     scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
  105: 
  106:     const alerts = readAlerts()
  107:     expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
  108:     expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
  109:   })
  110: 
  111:   it('detects Bearer token pattern', () => {
  112:     clearAlerts()
  113:     const parsed = makeTranscript(['Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.abcdefghij'])
  114:     scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
  115: 
  116:     const alerts = readAlerts()
  117:     expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
  118:     expect(alerts[0]!.type).toBe('sensitive_match')
  119:   })
  120: 
  121:   it('does not flag normal text without sensitive patterns', () => {
  122:     const parsed = makeTranscript([
  123:       'This is a normal conversation about code.',
  124:       'We discussed the API design for the project.',
  125:       'The function returns a list of tokens from the parser.',
  126:     ])
  127:     scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
  128: 
  129:     const alerts = readAlerts()
  130:     expect(alerts).toHaveLength(0)
  131:   })
  132: 
  133:   it('stops after first match (returns early)', () => {
  134:     const parsed = makeTranscript([
  135:       'api_key = first-key',
  136:       'password = second-secret',
  137:     ])
  138:     scanSensitive(parsed, '/tmp/project')
  139: 
  140:     // Should produce exactly one alert (returns after first sensitive match)
  141:     const alerts = readAlerts()
  142:     expect(alerts).toHaveLength(1)
  143:   })
  144: })
  145: 
  146: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  147: // SENSITIVE_PATTERNS 鈥?individual regex verification
  148: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  149: 
  150: describe('SENSITIVE_PATTERNS individual patterns', () => {
  151:   it('api_key pattern matches various forms', () => {
  152:     const apiKeyPattern = /(?:api[_-]?key|apikey)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
  153:     expect(apiKeyPattern.test('api_key = abc123')).toBe(true)
  154:     expect(apiKeyPattern.test('api-key: abc123')).toBe(true)
  155:     expect(apiKeyPattern.test('apikey=abc123')).toBe(true)
  156:     expect(apiKeyPattern.test('APIKEY = ABC')).toBe(true)
  157:     expect(apiKeyPattern.test('no match here')).toBe(false)
  158:   })
  159: 
  160:   it('password pattern matches password/passwd/pwd', () => {
  161:     const pwdPattern = /(?:password|passwd|pwd)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
  162:     expect(pwdPattern.test('password = secret')).toBe(true)
  163:     expect(pwdPattern.test('passwd: hidden')).toBe(true)
  164:     expect(pwdPattern.test('PWD=test123')).toBe(true)
  165:     expect(pwdPattern.test('no match')).toBe(false)
  166:   })
  167: 
  168:   it('secret/token pattern', () => {
  169:     const secretPattern = /(?:secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*\S+/i
  170:     expect(secretPattern.test('secret = mysecret')).toBe(true)
  171:     expect(secretPattern.test('TOKEN: mytoken')).toBe(true)
  172:     expect(secretPattern.test('no match')).toBe(false)
  173:   })
  174: 
  175:   it('known prefix pattern matches sk-, ghp_, glpat-, etc.', () => {
  176:     const prefixPattern = /(?:sk-|pk_live_|sk_live_|ghp_|gho_|glpat-)\S{10,}/
  177:     expect(prefixPattern.test('sk-abcdefghij1234567890')).toBe(true)
  178:     expect(prefixPattern.test('ghp_1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
  179:     expect(prefixPattern.test('pk_live_xxxxxxxxxx')).toBe(true)
  180:     expect(prefixPattern.test('sk_live_1234567890')).toBe(true)
  181:     expect(prefixPattern.test('gho_1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
  182:     expect(prefixPattern.test('glpat-1234567890abcdef')).toBe(true)
  183:     expect(prefixPattern.test('sk-short')).toBe(false) // too short
  184:     expect(prefixPattern.test('normal text')).toBe(false)
  185:   })
  186: 
  187:   it('Bearer token pattern matches long bearer strings', () => {
  188:     const bearerPattern = /Bearer\s+\S{20,}/i
  189:     expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9abc')).toBe(true)
  190:     expect(bearerPattern.test('bearer AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA')).toBe(true)
  191:     expect(bearerPattern.test('Bearer short')).toBe(false)
  192:     expect(bearerPattern.test('no bearer here')).toBe(false)
  193:   })
  194: })

Using Node v20.20.1
    1: import { beforeEach, afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
    2: import { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    3: import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
    4: import { join } from 'node:path'
    5: 
    6: import {
    7:   projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath,
    8:   syncProjectContextToMemory,
    9:   syncProjectOutputsToDevelopment,
   10: } from '../../src/heartbeat/sync.js'
   11: import type { ProjectEntry } from '../../src/heartbeat/config.js'
   12: 
   13: let sandbox: string
   14: let developmentPath: string
   15: let memoryPath: string
   16: 
   17: beforeEach(() => {
   18:   sandbox = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'memorytree-sync-'))
   19:   developmentPath = join(sandbox, 'dev')
   20:   memoryPath = join(sandbox, 'memory')
   21:   mkdirSync(developmentPath, { recursive: true })
   22:   mkdirSync(memoryPath, { recursive: true })
   23: })
   24: 
   25: afterEach(() => {
   26:   rmSync(sandbox, { recursive: true, force: true })
   27: })
   28: 
   29: describe('projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath', () => {
   30:   it('returns false when development and memory paths are the same', () => {
   31:     expect(projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath(makeProject(developmentPath, developmentPath))).toBe(false)
   32:   })
   33: })
   34: 
   35: describe('syncProjectContextToMemory', () => {
   36:   it('copies AGENTS and Memory/01-05 into the memory worktree and removes stale managed files', () => {
   37:     writeFileSync(join(developmentPath, 'AGENTS.md'), '# repo policy\n', 'utf-8')
   38:     mkdirSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '01_goals'), { recursive: true })
   39:     mkdirSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '02_todos'), { recursive: true })
   40:     writeFileSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '01_goals', 'goal.md'), 'goal\n', 'utf-8')
   41:     writeFileSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '02_todos', 'todo.md'), 'todo\n', 'utf-8')
   42: 
   43:     mkdirSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '05_archive'), { recursive: true })
   44:     writeFileSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '05_archive', 'stale.md'), 'stale\n', 'utf-8')
   45: 
   46:     const stats = syncProjectContextToMemory(makeProject(developmentPath, memoryPath))
   47: 
   48:     expect(readFileSync(join(memoryPath, 'AGENTS.md'), 'utf-8')).toContain('# repo policy')
   49:     expect(readFileSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '01_goals', 'goal.md'), 'utf-8')).toBe('goal\n')
   50:     expect(readFileSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '02_todos', 'todo.md'), 'utf-8')).toBe('todo\n')
   51:     expect(existsSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '05_archive', 'stale.md'))).toBe(false)
   52:     expect(stats.copied).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3)
   53:     expect(stats.deleted).toBe(1)
   54:   })
   55: })
   56: 
   57: describe('syncProjectOutputsToDevelopment', () => {
   58:   it('copies Memory/06-07 back into the development directory and prunes stale output files', () => {
   59:     mkdirSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'clean'), { recursive: true })
   60:     mkdirSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '07_reports'), { recursive: true })
   61:     writeFileSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'clean', 'session.md'), 'session\n', 'utf-8')
   62:     writeFileSync(join(memoryPath, 'Memory', '07_reports', 'index.html'), '<html></html>\n', 'utf-8')
   63: 
   64:     mkdirSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '07_reports', 'old'), { recursive: true })
   65:     writeFileSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '07_reports', 'old', 'stale.html'), 'stale\n', 'utf-8')
   66: 
   67:     const stats = syncProjectOutputsToDevelopment(makeProject(developmentPath, memoryPath))
   68: 
   69:     expect(readFileSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'clean', 'session.md'), 'utf-8')).toBe('session\n')
   70:     expect(readFileSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '07_reports', 'index.html'), 'utf-8')).toBe('<html></html>\n')
   71:     expect(existsSync(join(developmentPath, 'Memory', '07_reports', 'old', 'stale.html'))).toBe(false)
   72:     expect(stats.copied).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2)
   73:     expect(stats.deleted).toBe(1)
   74:   })
   75: })
   76: 
   77: function makeProject(devPath: string, memPath: string): ProjectEntry {
   78:   return {
   79:     id: 'demo',
   80:     path: memPath,
   81:     name: 'demo',
   82:     development_path: devPath,
   83:     memory_path: memPath,
   84:     memory_branch: 'memorytree',
   85:     heartbeat_interval: '5m',
   86:     refresh_interval: '30m',
   87:     auto_push: true,
   88:     generate_report: true,
   89:     ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   90:     locale: 'en',
   91:     gh_pages_branch: '',
   92:     cname: '',
   93:     webhook_url: '',
   94:     report_base_url: '',
   95:     report_port: 10010,
   96:     last_heartbeat_at: '',
   97:     last_refresh_at: '',
   98:   }
   99: }
