  443:       'register',
  444:       '--root', repoRoot,
  445:       '--quick-start',
  446:     ], {
  447:       cwd: repoRoot,
  448:       env: isolatedEnv(homeDir),
  449:     })
  450:     assertSuccess(result…1625 tokens truncated…goal-summary', 'Build durable memory workflows.',
  594:       '--locale', 'en',
  595:       '--date', '2026-03-17',
  596:       '--time', '10:30',
  597:       '--format', 'json',
  598:     ])
  599:     assertSuccess(upgradeResult, 'memorytree upgrade (memorytree branch)')
  600: 
  601:     writeFileSync(join(repoRoot, '.gitignore'), 'Memory/07_reports/\n', 'utf-8')
  602:     commitAll(repoRoot, 'chore: scaffold memorytree workspace')
  603: 
  604:     writeConfig(homeDir, {
  605:       projectPath: repoRoot,
  606:       autoPush: false,
  607:       generateReport: true,
  608:     })
  609: 
  610:     writeCodexTranscript({
  611:       homeDir,
  612:       repoPath: repoRoot,
  613:       branch: 'memorytree/e2e',
  614:       stem: 'heartbeat-commit',
  615:     })
  616: 
  617:     const result = runCli(['daemon', 'run-once'], {
  618:       cwd: repoRoot,
  619:       env: isolatedEnv(homeDir),
  620:     })
  621:     assertSuccess(result, 'memorytree daemon run-once (memorytree branch)')
  622: 
  623:     expect(runGit(['log', '-1', '--pretty=%s'], repoRoot).stdout.trim()).toBe(
  624:       'memorytree(transcripts): import 1 transcript(s)',
  625:     )
  626:     expect(listFiles(join(repoRoot, 'Memory', '06_transcripts', 'manifests')).length).toBe(1)
  627:     expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, 'Memory', '07_reports', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
  628:     expect(runGit(['status', '--short'], repoRoot).stdout.trim()).toBe('?? Memory/06_transcripts/raw/')
  629:     expect(listFiles(join(homeDir, '.memorytree', 'transcripts', 'index')).length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
  630:   })
  631: 
  632:   it('keeps repository branches clean during heartbeat on non-memorytree branches', () => {
  633:     expect(existsSync(cliPath)).toBe(true)
  634:     initGitRepo(repoRoot, 'main')
  635:     const beforeHead = runGit(['rev-parse', 'HEAD'], repoRoot).stdout.trim()
  636: 
  637:     writeConfig(homeDir, {
  638:       projectPath: repoRoot,
  639:       autoPush: false,
  640:       generateReport: false,
  641:     })
  642: 
  643:     writeCodexTranscript({
  644:       homeDir,
  645:       repoPath: repoRoot,
  646:       branch: 'main',
  647:       stem: 'heartbeat-global-only',
  648:     })
  649: 
  650:     const result = runCli(['daemon', 'run-once'], {
  651:       cwd: repoRoot,
  652:       env: isolatedEnv(homeDir),
  653:     })
  654:     assertSuccess(result, 'memorytree daemon run-once (main branch)')
  655: 
  656:     const afterHead = runGit(['rev-parse', 'HEAD'], repoRoot).stdout.trim()
  657:     expect(afterHead).toBe(beforeHead)
  658:     expect(runGit(['status', '--short'], repoRoot).stdout.trim()).toBe('')
  659:     expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, 'Memory', '06_transcripts'))).toBe(false)
  660:     expect(listFiles(join(homeDir, '.memorytree', 'transcripts', 'index', 'manifests')).length).toBe(1)
  661:   })
  662: 
  663:   it('deploys heartbeat reports to a gh-pages branch with CNAME', () => {
  664:     expect(existsSync(cliPath)).toBe(true)
  665:     initGitRepo(repoRoot, 'memorytree/e2e')
  666: 
  667:     const bareRemote = join(sandboxRoot, 'remote.git')
  668:     initBareRemote(bareRemote)
  669:     assertSuccess(runGit(['remote', 'add', 'origin', bareRemote], repoRoot), 'git remote add origin')
  670: 
  671:     const upgradeResult = runCli([
  672:       'upgrade',
  673:       '--root', repoRoot,
  674:       '--project-name', 'repo',
  675:       '--goal-summary', 'Build durable memory workflows.',
  676:       '--locale', 'en',
  677:       '--date', '2026-03-17',
  678:       '--time', '10:30',
  679:       '--format', 'json',
  680:     ])
  681:     assertSuccess(upgradeResult, 'memorytree upgrade for gh-pages deploy')
  682: 
  683:     writeFileSync(join(repoRoot, '.gitignore'), 'Memory/07_reports/\n', 'utf-8')
  684:     commitAll(repoRoot, 'chore: scaffold memorytree workspace')
  685: 
  686:     writeConfig(homeDir, {
  687:       projectPath: repoRoot,
  688:       autoPush: false,
  689:       generateReport: true,
  690:       ghPagesBranch: 'gh-pages',
  691:       cname: 'memory.example.com',
  692:       reportBaseUrl: 'https://memory.example.com',
  693:     })
  694: 
  695:     writeCodexTranscript({
  696:       homeDir,
  697:       repoPath: repoRoot,
  698:       branch: 'memorytree/e2e',
  699:       stem: 'heartbeat-gh-pages',
  700:     })
  701: 
  702:     const result = runCli(['daemon', 'run-once'], {
  703:       cwd: repoRoot,
  704:       env: isolatedEnv(homeDir),
  705:     })
  706:     assertSuccess(result, 'memorytree daemon run-once with gh-pages deploy')
  707: 
  708:     expect(runGitDir(['rev-parse', 'gh-pages'], bareRemote).status).toBe(0)
  709:     expect(runGitDir(['show', 'gh-pages:CNAME'], bareRemote).stdout.trim()).toBe('memory.example.com')
  710: 
  711:     const tree = runGitDir(['ls-tree', '--name-only', '-r', 'gh-pages'], bareRemote)
  712:     expect(tree.stdout).toContain('index.html')
  713:     expect(tree.stdout).toContain('transcripts/index.html')
  714:     expect(tree.stdout).toContain('feed.xml')
  715: 
  716:     const feed = runGitDir(['show', 'gh-pages:feed.xml'], bareRemote)
  717:     expect(feed.stdout).toContain('https://memory.example.com')
  718:   })
  719: 
  720:   it('keeps heartbeat successful when webhook delivery is rejected', () => {
  721:     expect(existsSync(cliPath)).toBe(true)
  722:     initGitRepo(repoRoot, 'memorytree/e2e')
  723: 
  724:     const upgradeResult = runCli([
  725:       'upgrade',
  726:       '--root', repoRoot,
  727:       '--project-name', 'repo',
  728:       '--goal-summary', 'Build durable memory workflows.',
  729:       '--locale', 'en',
  730:       '--date', '2026-03-17',
  731:       '--time', '10:30',
  732:       '--format', 'json',
  733:     ])
  734:     assertSuccess(upgradeResult, 'memorytree upgrade for webhook resilience')
  735: 
  736:     writeFileSync(join(repoRoot, '.gitignore'), 'Memory/07_reports/\n', 'utf-8')
  737:     commitAll(repoRoot, 'chore: scaffold memorytree workspace')
  738: 
  739:     writeConfig(homeDir, {
  740:       projectPath: repoRoot,
  741:       autoPush: false,
  742:       generateReport: true,
  743:       webhookUrl: 'https://127.0.0.1/hook',
  744:       reportBaseUrl: 'https://memory.example.com',
  745:     })
  746: 
  747:     writeCodexTranscript({
  748:       homeDir,
  749:       repoPath: repoRoot,
  750:       branch: 'memorytree/e2e',
  751:       stem: 'heartbeat-webhook',
  752:     })
  753: 
  754:     const result = runCli(['daemon', 'run-once'], {
  755:       cwd: repoRoot,
  756:       env: isolatedEnv(homeDir),
  757:     })
  758:     assertSuccess(result, 'memorytree daemon run-once with rejected webhook')
  759: 
  760:     expect(existsSync(join(repoRoot, 'Memory', '07_reports', 'index.html'))).toBe(true)
  761:     expect(runGit(['log', '-1', '--pretty=%s'], repoRoot).stdout.trim()).toBe(
  762:       'memorytree(transcripts): import 1 transcript(s)',
  763:     )
  764:   })
  765: })
  766: 
  767: function runCli(args: readonly string[], options: RunOptions = {}): CommandResult {
  768:   return runCommand(process.execPath, [cliPath, ...args], options)
  769: }
  770: 
  771: function runGit(args: readonly string[], cwd: string, env?: NodeJS.ProcessEnv): CommandResult {
  772:   return runCommand('git', args, { cwd, env })
  773: }
  774: 
  775: function runGitDir(args: readonly string[], gitDir: string): CommandResult {
  776:   return runCommand('git', ['--git-dir', gitDir, ...args], {})
  777: }
  778: 
  779: function runCommand(command: string, args: readonly string[], options: RunOptions): CommandResult {
  780:   const result = spawnSync(command, [...args], {
  781:     cwd: options.cwd,
  782:     env: {
  783:       ...process.env,
  784:       ...options.env,
  785:     },
  786:     encoding: 'utf-8',
  787:     stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
  788:     timeout: 120_000,
  789:   })
  790: 
  791:   if (result.error) {
  792:     throw result.error
  793:   }
  794: 
  795:   return {
  796:     status: result.status,
  797:     stdout: result.stdout ?? '',
  798:     stderr: result.stderr ?? '',
  799:   }
  800: }
  801: 
  802: async function startCli(
  803:   args: readonly string[],
  804:   options: RunOptions & { waitForUrl?: string } = {},
  805: ): Promise<RunningProcess> {
  806:   let stdout = ''
  807:   let stderr = ''
  808: 
  809:   const child = spawn(process.execPath, [cliPath, ...args], {
  810:     cwd: options.cwd,
  811:     env: {
  812:       ...process.env,
  813:       ...options.env,
  814:     },
  815:     stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
  816:   })
  817: 
  818:   liveProcesses.push(child)
  819:   child.stdout.on('data', chunk => {
  820:     stdout += chunk.toString()
  821:   })
  822:   child.stderr.on('data', chunk => {
  823:     stderr += chunk.toString()
  824:   })
  825: 
  826:   await waitFor(async () => {
  827:     if (child.exitCode !== null) {
  828:       throw new Error([
  829:         `process exited early with code ${String(child.exitCode)}`,
  830:         'stdout:',
  831:         stdout.trim(),
  832:         'stderr:',
  833:         stderr.trim(),
  834:       ].join('\n'))
  835:     }
  836: 
  837:     if (!options.waitForUrl) {
  838:       return stdout.length > 0
  839:     }
  840: 
  841:     try {
  842:       const response = await fetch(options.waitForUrl)
  843:       return response.status === 200
  844:     } catch {
  845:       return false
  846:     }
  847:   }, 15_000)
  848: 
  849:   return {
  850:     child,
  851:     stdout: () => stdout,
  852:     stderr: () => stderr,
  853:   }
  854: }
  855: 
  856: function assertSuccess(result: CommandResult, label: string): void {
  857:   if (result.status !== 0) {
  858:     throw new Error([
  859:       `${label} failed with exit code ${String(result.status)}`,
  860:       'stdout:',
  861:       result.stdout.trim(),
  862:       'stderr:',
  863:       result.stderr.trim(),
  864:     ].join('\n'))
  865:   }
  866: }
  867: 
  868: function isolatedEnv(homePath: string): NodeJS.ProcessEnv {
  869:   return {
  870:     HOME: homePath,
  871:     USERPROFILE: homePath,
  872:   }
  873: }
  874: 
  875: function initGitRepo(root: string, branch: string): void {
  876:   mkdirSync(root, { recursive: true })
  877:   assertSuccess(runGit(['init', '-b', branch], root), 'git init')
  878:   assertSuccess(runGit(['config', 'user.email', 'e2e@example.com'], root), 'git config user.email')
  879:   assertSuccess(runGit(['config', 'user.name', 'MemoryTree E2E'], root), 'git config user.name')
  880: 
  881:   writeFileSync(join(root, 'README.md'), '# E2E Repo\n', 'utf-8')
  882:   assertSuccess(runGit(['add', 'README.md'], root), 'git add README')
  883:   assertSuccess(runGit(['commit', '-m', 'chore: init repo'], root), 'git commit init')
  884: }
  885: 
  886: function initBareRemote(root: string): void {
  887:   assertSuccess(
  888:     runCommand('git', ['init', '--bare', root], { cwd: sandboxRoot }),
  889:     'git init --bare',
  890:   )
  891: }
  892: 
  893: function commitAll(root: string, message: string): void {
  894:   assertSuccess(runGit(['add', '.'], root), 'git add .')
  895:   assertSuccess(runGit(['commit', '-m', message], root), `git commit ${message}`)
  896: }
  897: 
  898: function writeConfig(
  899:   homePath: string,
  900:   options: {
  901:     projectPath: string
  902:     autoPush: boolean
  903:     generateReport: boolean
  904:     ghPagesBranch?: string
  905:     cname?: string
  906:     webhookUrl?: string
  907:     reportBaseUrl?: string
  908:     reportPort?: number
  909:   },
  910: ): void {
  911:   const configDir = join(homePath, '.memorytree')
  912:   mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
  913: 
  914:   const configToml = [
  915:     'heartbeat_interval = "5m"',
  916:     `auto_push = ${options.autoPush ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
  917:     'log_level = "debug"',
  918:     `generate_report = ${options.generateReport ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
  919:     'ai_summary_model = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"',
  920:     'locale = "en"',
  921:     `gh_pages_branch = "${escapeToml(options.ghPagesBranch ?? '')}"`,
  922:     `cname = "${escapeToml(options.cname ?? '')}"`,
  923:     `webhook_url = "${escapeToml(options.webhookUrl ?? '')}"`,
  924:     `report_base_url = "${escapeToml(options.reportBaseUrl ?? '')}"`,
  925:     `report_port = ${String(options.reportPort ?? 10010)}`,
  926:     'watch_dirs = []',
  927:     '',
  928:     '[[projects]]',
  929:     `path = "${escapeToml(toPosix(options.projectPath))}"`,
  930:     'name = "repo"',
  931:     '',
  932:   ].join('\n')
  933: 
  934:   writeFileSync(join(configDir, 'config.toml'), configToml, 'utf-8')
  935: }
  936: 
  937: function writeCodexTranscript(
  938:   options: {
  939:     homeDir: string
  940:     repoPath: string
  941:     branch: string
  942:     stem: string
  943:     startedAt?: string
  944:     title?: string
  945:     userText?: string
  946:     assistantText?: string
  947:   },
  948: ): string {
  949:   const transcriptDir = join(options.homeDir, '.codex', 'sessions', 'e2e')
  950:   mkdirSync(transcriptDir, { recursive: true })
  951: 
  952:   const startedAt = options.startedAt ?? '2026-03-17T10:30:00Z'
  953:   const transcriptPath = join(transcriptDir, `${options.stem}.jsonl`)
  954:   const repoPath = toPosix(options.repoPath)
  955:   const records = [
  956:     {
  957:       type: 'session_meta',
  958:       payload: {
  959:         id: `sess-${options.stem}`,
  960:         title: options.title ?? `E2E ${options.stem}`,
  961:         cwd: repoPath,
  962:         git: { branch: options.branch },
  963:         timestamp: startedAt,
  964:       },
  965:     },
  966:     {
  967:       type: 'response_item',
  968:       timestamp: offsetIso(startedAt, 1),
  969:       payload: {
  970:         type: 'message',
  971:         role: 'user',
  972:         content: [{ type: 'input_text', text: options.userText ?? 'Capture this coding session.' }],
  973:       },
  974:     },
  975:     {
  976:       type: 'response_item',
  977:       timestamp: offsetIso(startedAt, 2),
  978:       payload: {
  979:         type: 'message',
  980:         role: 'assistant',
  981:         content: [{ type: 'text', text: options.assistantText ?? 'Captured and indexed.' }],
  982:       },
  983:     },
  984:     {
  985:       type: 'response_item',
  986:       timestamp: offsetIso(startedAt, 3),
  987:       payload: {
  988:         type: 'function_call',
  989:         name: 'read_file',
  990:         arguments: { path: 'README.md' },
  991:       },
  992:     },
  993:     {
  994:       type: 'response_item',
  995:       timestamp: offsetIso(startedAt, 4),
  996:       payload: {
  997:         type: 'function_call_output',
  998:         name: 'read_file',
  999:         output: '# E2E Repo',
 1000:       },
 1001:     },
 1002:   ]
 1003: 
 1004:   writeFileSync(
 1005:     transcriptPath,
 1006:     records.map(record => JSON.stringify(record)).join('\n') + '\n',
 1007:     'utf-8',
 1008:   )
 1009:   return transcriptPath
 1010: }
 1011: 
 1012: function listFiles(root: string): string[] {
 1013:   if (!existsSync(root)) return []
 1014: 
 1015:   const files: string[] = []
 1016:   const stack = [root]
 1017: 
 1018:   while (stack.length > 0) {
 1019:     const current = stack.pop()
 1020:     if (!current) continue
 1021: 
 1022:     for (const entry of readdirSync(current)) {
 1023:       const entryPath = join(current, entry)
 1024:       const stats = statSync(entryPath)
 1025:       if (stats.isDirectory()) {
 1026:         stack.push(entryPath)
 1027:       } else {
 1028:         files.push(toPosix(relative(root, entryPath)))
 1029:       }
 1030:     }
 1031:   }
 1032: 
 1033:   return files.sort()
 1034: }
 1035: 
 1036: async function getAvailablePort(): Promise<number> {
 1037:   return await new Promise<number>((resolvePort, reject) => {
 1038:     const server = createServer()
 1039:     server.unref()
 1040:     server.on('error', reject)
 1041:     server.listen(0, '127.0.0.1', () => {
 1042:       const address = server.address()
 1043:       if (address === null || typeof address === 'string') {
 1044:         server.close()
 1045:         reject(new Error('failed to allocate port'))
 1046:         return
 1047:       }
 1048: 
 1049:       server.close(error => {
 1050:         if (error) {
 1051:           reject(error)
 1052:           return
 1053:         }
 1054:         resolvePort(address.port)
 1055:       })
 1056:     })
 1057:   })
 1058: }
 1059: 
 1060: async function stopProcess(child: ChildProcessWithoutNullStreams): Promise<void> {
 1061:   if (child.exitCode !== null) {
 1062:     return
 1063:   }
 1064: 
 1065:   try {
 1066:     child.kill()
 1067:   } catch {
 1068:     return
 1069:   }
 1070: 
 1071:   await new Promise<void>(resolve => {
 1072:     const timeout = setTimeout(resolve, 2_000)
 1073:     const done = () => {
 1074:       clearTimeout(timeout)
 1075:       resolve()
 1076:     }
 1077:     child.once('exit', done)
 1078:     child.once('close', done)
 1079:   })
 1080: }
 1081: 
 1082: async function removeTreeWithRetry(root: string): Promise<void> {
 1083:   const deadline = Date.now() + 5_000
 1084: 
 1085:   // Windows can keep directory handles alive briefly after child.exit.
 1086:   let removed = false
 1087:   while (!removed) {
 1088:     try {
 1089:       rmSync(root, { recursive: true, force: true })
 1090:       removed = true
 1091:     } catch (error: unknown) {
 1092:       const err = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException
 1093:       if (!err.code || Date.now() >= deadline) {
 1094:         throw error
 1095:       }
 1096: 
 1097:       const code = String(err.code)
 1098:       if (code !== 'EBUSY' && code !== 'ENOTEMPTY' && code !== 'EPERM') {
 1099:         throw error
 1100:       }
 1101: 
 1102:       await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100))
 1103:     }
 1104:   }
 1105: }
 1106: 
 1107: async function waitFor(
 1108:   predicate: () => boolean | Promise<boolean>,
 1109:   timeoutMs: number,
 1110:   intervalMs = 100,
 1111: ): Promise<void> {
 1112:   const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs
 1113:   let lastError: unknown = null
 1114: 
 1115:   while (Date.now() < deadline) {
 1116:     try {
 1117:       if (await predicate()) {
 1118:         return
 1119:       }
 1120:       lastError = null
 1121:     } catch (error) {
 1122:       lastError = error
 1123:     }
 1124: 
 1125:     await new Promise(resolveSleep => setTimeout(resolveSleep, intervalMs))
 1126:   }
 1127: 
 1128:   if (lastError instanceof Error) {
 1129:     throw lastError
 1130:   }
 1131:   if (lastError !== null) {
 1132:     throw new Error(String(lastError))
 1133:   }
 1134:   throw new Error('timed out waiting for condition')
 1135: }
 1136: 
 1137: function offsetIso(baseIso: string, seconds: number): string {
 1138:   return new Date(Date.parse(baseIso) + seconds * 1000).toISOString().replace(/\.\d{3}Z$/, 'Z')
 1139: }
 1140: 
 1141: function toPosix(value: string): string {
 1142:   return value.replace(/\\/g, '/')
 1143: }
 1144: 
 1145: function escapeToml(value: string): string {
 1146:   return value.replace(/\\/g, '\\\\').replace(/"/g, '\\"')
 1147: }

  353:       name: 'repo',
  354:       development_path: '/repo',
  355:       memory_path: '/memorytree/custom-worktree',
  356:       memory_branch: 'custom-memorytree',
  357:       heartbeat_interval: '15m',
  358:       refresh_interval: '45m',
  359:       auto_push: false,
  360:       generate_report: false,
  361:       ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
  362:       locale: 'en',
  363:       gh_pages_branch: '',
  364:       cname: '',
  365:       webhook_url: '',
  366:       report_base_url: '',
  367:       report_port: 12000,
  368:       last_heartbeat_at: '',
  369:       last_refresh_at: '',
  370:     }
  371:     const currentConfig = {
  372:       heartbeat_interval: '9m',
  373:       auto_push: true,
  374:       projects: [existingProject],
  375:       watch_dirs: [],
  376:       log_level: 'info',
  377:       generate_report: false,
  378:       ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
  379:       locale: 'en',
  380:       gh_pages_branch: '',
  381:       cname: '',
  382:       webhook_url: '',
  383:       report_base_url: '',
  384:       report_port: 10010,
  385:     }
  386: 
  387:     vi.doMock('../../src/heartbeat/config.js', () => ({
  388:       DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH: 'memorytree',
  389:       configPath: () => '/nonexistent/config.toml',
  390:       loadConfig: () => currentConfig,
  391:       saveConfig: (cfg: unknown) => { savedConfigs.push(cfg) },
  392:       intervalToSeconds: () => 300,
  393:       findProjectForPath: (cfg: typeof currentConfig) => cfg.projects[0] ?? null,
  394:       upsertProject: (cfg: typeof currentConfig, _root: string, overrides: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
  395:         ...cfg,
  396:         projects: [
  397:           {
  398:             ...existingProject,
  399:             ...overrides,
  400:           },
  401:         ],
  402:       }),
  403:     }))
  404:     vi.doMock('../../src/heartbeat/lock.js', () => ({
  405:       readLockPid: () => null,
  406:     }))
  407:     vi.doMock('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js', () => ({
  408:       defaultProjectWorktreePath: () => '/memorytree/worktrees/repo',
  409:       defaultProjectWorktreeBranch: () => 'memorytree',
  410:       ensureBranchUpstream: () => ({ remote: 'origin', created: true }),
  411:       ensureProjectWorktree: () => ({ branch: 'custom-memorytree', created: false }),
  412:       isValidWorktreeBranchName: () => true,
  413:     }))
  414:     vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
  415:       execCommand: () => '',
  416:     }))
  417: 
  418:     const mod = await import('../../src/cli/cmd-daemon.js')
  419:     const result = mod.cmdRegisterProject({
  420:       root: '/repo',
  421:       quickStart: true,
  422:     })
  423: 
  424:     expect(result).toBe(0)
  425:     expect(savedConfigs).toHaveLength(1)
  426:     expect(savedConfigs[0]).toMatchObject({
  427:       projects: [
  428:         expect.objectContaining({
  429:           memory_path: '/memorytree/custom-worktree',
  430:           memory_branch: 'custom-memorytree',
  431:           heartbeat_interval: '15m',
  432:           refresh_interval: '45m',
  433:           auto_push: false,
  434:           generate_report: false,
  435:           report_port: 12000,
  436:         }),
  437:       ],
  438:     })
  439:     expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Memory branch: custom-memorytree')
  440:     expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Heartbeat interval: 15m')
  441:     expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Refresh interval: 45m')
  442:     expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Auto-push: false')
  443:     expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Generate report: false')
  444:     expect(stdoutChunks.join('')).toContain('Upstream configured: skipped (auto_push disabled)')
  445:   })
  446: })
  447: 
  448: describe('cmdQuickStart', () => {
  449:   let stdoutChunks: string[]
  450:   let stderrChunks: string[]
  451:   const originalWrite = process.stdout.write
  452:   const originalStderrWrite = process.stderr.write
  453: 
  454:   beforeEach(() => {
  455:     stdoutChunks = []
  456:     stderrChunks = []
  457:     process.stdout.write = ((chunk: string) => {
  458:       stdoutChunks.push(chunk)
  459:       return true
  460:     }) as typeof process.stdout.write

{
  "turn_id": "019d18f6-3d15-7fe2-a22d-edc7463689cd",
  "cwd": "d:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow",
  "current_date": "2026-03-23",
  "timezone": "Asia/Shanghai",
  "approval_policy": "never",
  "sandbox_policy": {
    "type": "danger-full-access"
  },
  "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
  "personality": "friendly",
  "collaboration_mode": {
    "mode": "default",
    "settings": {
      "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
      "reasoning_effort": "high",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "high",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\r\n\r\n## Read Order\r\n- Read this file first.\r\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\r\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\r\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\r\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\r\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\r\n\r\n## Memory Rules\r\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\r\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\r\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\r\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\r\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\r\n\r\n## Transcript Rules\r\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\r\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\r\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\r\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\r\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: approved on 2026-03-21.\r\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\r\n\r\n## Git Rules\r\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\r\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\r\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\r\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\r\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\r\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\r\n\r\n## Heartbeat Rules\r\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\r\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\r\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\r\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\r\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\r\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\r\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

 223:     try {

 231:   if (importedCount === 0) {
 232:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] No new transcripts to import.`)
 233:     return
 234:   }
 235: 
 236:   logger.info(`[${projectName}] Imported ${importedCount} transcript(s).`)
 237:   if (mirrorToRepo) {
 238:     gitCommitAndPush({ auto_push: autoPush }, projectPath, projectName, importedCount)
 239:   } else {
 240:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] Skipped repo-local commit/push on branch '${branch}'.`)
 241:   }
 242: 
 243:   if (generateReport) {
 244:     try {
 245:       const { buildReport } = await import('../report/build.js')
 246:       await buildReport({
 247:         root: projectPath,
 248:         output: join(projectPath, 'Memory', '07_reports'),
 249:         noAi: !process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'],
 250:         model: aiSummaryModel,
 251:         locale,
 252:         ghPagesBranch,
 253:         cname,
 254:         webhookUrl,
 255:         reportBaseUrl,
 256:         ...(extraManifestDirs ? { extraManifestDirs } : {}),
 257:       })
 258:       logger.info(`[${projectName}] Report generated.`)
 259:     } catch (err: unknown) {
 260:       logger.warn(`[${projectName}] Report generation failed: ${String(err)}`)
 261:       // Never propagate 鈥?report failure must not abort heartbeat cycle
 262:     }
 263:   }
 264: }
 265: 
 266: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 267: // Sensitive pattern scanning
 268: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 269: 
 270: export function scanSensitive(parsed: ParsedTranscript, projectPath: string): void {
 271:   const logger = getLogger()
 272:   for (const msg of parsed.messages) {
 273:     for (const pattern of SENSITIVE_PATTERNS) {
 274:       if (pattern.test(msg.text)) {
 275:         logger.warn(
 276:           `Sensitive pattern detected in transcript ${parsed.source_path} (project: ${basename(projectPath)}, role: ${msg.role})`,
 277:         )
 278:         writeAlert(
 279:           toPosixPath(projectPath),
 280:           'sensitive_match',
 281:           `Sensitive pattern in transcript: ${basename(parsed.source_path)}`,
 282:         )
 283:         return
 284:       }
 285:     }
 286:   }
 287: }
 288: 
 289: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 290: // Git operations
 291: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 292: 
 293: export function gitCommitAndPush(
 294:   config: Pick<Config, 'auto_push'>,
 295:   projectPath: string,
 296:   projectName: string,
 297:   count: number,
 298: ): void {
 299:   const logger = getLogger()
 300: 
 301:   const changedPaths = changedMemoryPaths(projectPath)
 302:   if (changedPaths.length === 0) {
 303:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] No git changes in Memory/.`)
 304:     return
 305:   }
 306: 
 307:   const stageablePaths = changedPaths.filter(path => !isRepoRawTranscriptPath(path))
 308:   if (stageablePaths.length === 0) {
 309:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] Only raw transcript mirror changes detected; skipping commit.`)
 310:     return
 311:   }
 312: 
 313:   git(projectPath, 'add', '--', ...stageablePaths)
 314:   git(projectPath, 'commit', '-m', `memorytree(transcripts): import ${count} transcript(s)`)
 315:   logger.info(`[${projectName}] Committed ${count} transcript import(s).`)
 316: 
 317:   if (!config.auto_push) {
 318:     logger.info(`[${projectName}] auto_push disabled, skipping push.`)
 319:     return
 320:   }
 321: 
 322:   const remotes = git(projectPath, 'remote')
 323:   if (!remotes.trim()) {
 324:     logger.warn(`[${projectName}] No git remote configured, skipping push.`)
 325:     writeAlert(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'no_remote', 'Push skipped: no Git remote configured.')
 326:     return
 327:   }
 328: 
 329:   if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
 330:     logger.warn(`[${projectName}] Push failed, retrying once...`)
 331:     if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
 332:       logger.error(`[${projectName}] Push failed after retry.`)
 333:       writeAlertWithThreshold(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed', 'Push failed after retry.')
 334:       return
 335:     }
 336:   }
 337: 
 338:   resetFailureCount(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed')
 339: }
 340: 
 341: export function tryPush(projectPath: string, projectName: string): boolean {
 342:   try {
 343:     const branch = currentBranch(projectPath)
 344:     if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
 345:       git(projectPath, 'push')
 346:     } else {
 347:       const upstream = ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath, branch)
 348:       if (upstream.remote === null) {
 349:         return false
 350:       }
 351:       getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Configured upstream ${upstream.remote}/${branch}.`)
 352:     }
 353:     getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully.`)
 354:     return true
 355:   } catch {
 356:     return false
 357:   }
 358: }
 359: 
 360: export function currentBranch(projectPath: string): string {
 361:   return git(projectPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim()
 362: }
 363: 
 364: export function isDedicatedMemorytreeBranch(
 365:   branch: string,
 366:   project?: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>,
 367: ): boolean {
 368:   return isProjectMemoryBranch(branch, project)
 369: }
 370: 
 371: export function changedMemoryPaths(projectPath: string): string[] {
 372:   const status = git(projectPath, 'status', '--porcelain', '--untracked-files=all', '--', 'Memory/')
 373:   const seen = new Set<string>()
 374:   const paths: string[] = []
 375: 
 376:   for (const rawLine of status.split(/\r?\n/)) {
 377:     const path = statusLinePath(rawLine)
 378:     if (path === null || seen.has(path)) continue
 379:     seen.add(path)
 380:     paths.push(path)
 381:   }
 382: 
 383:   return paths
 384: }
 385: 
 386: export function isRepoRawTranscriptPath(path: string): boolean {
 387:   return path.replace(/\\/g, '/').startsWith(RAW_TRANSCRIPT_PREFIX)
 388: }
 389: 
 390: function statusLinePath(line: string): string | null {
 391:   if (line.length < 4) return null
 392: 
 393:   const rawPath = line.slice(3).trim()
 394:   if (!rawPath) return null
 395: 
 396:   const path = rawPath.includes(' -> ')
 397:     ? rawPath.slice(rawPath.lastIndexOf(' -> ') + 4)
 398:     : rawPath
 399: 
 400:   return unquotePath(path)
 401: }
 402: 
 403: function unquotePath(path: string): string {
 404:   if (!(path.startsWith('"') && path.endsWith('"'))) return path
 405: 
 406:   return path
 407:     .slice(1, -1)
 408:     .replace(/\\"/g, '"')
 409:     .replace(/\\\\/g, '\\')
 410: }

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
   1: import { existsSync, mkdirSync, realpathSync } from 'node:fs'
   2: import { basename, dirname, isAbsolute, resolve } from 'node:path'
   3: import { platform } from 'node:process'
   4: 
   5: import type { ProjectEntry } from './config.js'
   6: import { DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH, memorytreeRoot } from './config.js'
   7: import { execCommand, git } from '../utils/exec.js'
   8: 
   9: export interface WorktreeStatus {
  10:   readonly branch: string
  11:   readonly created: boolean
  12: }
  13: 
  14: export interface BranchUpstreamStatus {
  15:   readonly remote: string | null
  16:   readonly created: boolean
  17: }
  18: 
  19: export function defaultProjectWorktreePath(developmentPath: string): string {
  20:   return resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'worktrees', slugifySegment(basename(resolve(developmentPath))))
  21: }
  22: 
  23: export function defaultProjectWorktreeBranch(): string {
  24:   return DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
  25: }
  26: 
  27: export function resolveProjectWorktreeBranch(project: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>): string {
  28:   const requested = project.memory_branch.trim()
  29:   return isValidWorktreeBranchName(requested)
  30:     ? requested
  31:     : DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
  32: }
  33: 
  34: export function ensureProjectWorktree(project: ProjectEntry): WorktreeStatus {
  35:   const developmentPath = resolve(project.development_path)
  36:   const memoryPath = resolve(project.memory_path)
  37:   if (samePath(developmentPath, memoryPath)) {
  38:     return {
  39:       branch: git(memoryPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim(),
  40:       created: false,
  41:     }
  42:   }
  43: 
  44:   const repoRoot = git(developmentPath, 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel').trim()
  45:   const repoCommonDir = gitCommonDir(developmentPath)
  46:   const branch = resolveProjectWorktreeBranch(project)
  47: 
  48:   if (existsSync(memoryPath)) {
  49:     const existingRoot = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'], {
  50:       cwd: memoryPath,
  51:       allowFailure: true,
  52:     }).trim()
  53:     if (!existingRoot) {
  54:       throw new Error(`Configured memory_path is not a git worktree: ${memoryPath}`)
  55:     }
  56:     const existingCommonDir = gitCommonDir(memoryPath)
  57:     if (!samePath(existingCommonDir, repoCommonDir)) {
  58:       throw new Error(`Configured memory_path belongs to a different repository: ${memoryPath}`)
  59:     }
  60: 
  61:     const current = git(memoryPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim()
  62:     if (current !== branch) {
  63:       if (branchExists(repoRoot, branch)) {
  64:         git(memoryPath, 'checkout', branch)
  65:       } else {
  66:         git(memoryPath, 'checkout', '-b', branch)
  67:       }
  68:     }
  69: 
  70:     return { branch, created: false }
  71:   }
  72: 
  73:   mkdirSync(dirname(memoryPath), { recursive: true })
  74:   if (branchExists(repoRoot, branch)) {
  75:     git(repoRoot, 'worktree', 'add', memoryPath, branch)
  76:   } else {
  77:     git(repoRoot, 'worktree', 'add', '-b', branch, memoryPath, 'HEAD')
  78:   }
  79: 
  80:   return { branch, created: true }
  81: }
  82: 
  83: export function ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath: string, branch: string): BranchUpstreamStatus {
  84:   const remote = resolvePushRemote(projectPath)
  85:   if (remote === null) {
  86:     return { remote: null, created: false }
  87:   }
  88:   if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
  89:     return { remote, created: false }
  90:   }
  91: 
  92:   git(projectPath, 'push', '-u', remote, branch)
  93:   return { remote, created: true }
  94: }
  95: 
  96: export function resolvePushRemote(projectPath: string): string | null {
  97:   const remotes = git(projectPath, 'remote')
  98:     .split(/\r?\n/)
  99:     .map(remote => remote.trim())
 100:     .filter(Boolean)
 101: 
 102:   if (remotes.length === 0) {
 103:     return null
 104:   }
 105:   if (remotes.includes('origin')) {
 106:     return 'origin'
 107:   }
 108:   return remotes[0] ?? null
 109: }
 110: 
 111: export function hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath: string): boolean {
 112:   const output = execCommand(
 113:     'git',
 114:     ['rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', '--symbolic-full-name', '@{u}'],
 115:     { cwd: projectPath, allowFailure: true },
 116:   )
 117:   return output.trim().length > 0
 118: }
 119: 
 120: export function isValidWorktreeBranchName(branch: string): boolean {
 121:   if (!branch || branch.trim() !== branch) {
 122:     return false
 123:   }
 124: 
 125:   const output = execCommand('git', ['check-ref-format', '--branch', branch], { allowFailure: true })
 126:   return output.trim() === branch
 127: }
 128: 
 129: export function isProjectMemoryBranch(
 130:   branch: string,
 131:   project?: Pick<ProjectEntry, 'memory_branch'>,
 132: ): boolean {
 133:   const normalized = branch.trim()
 134:   if (!normalized) return false
 135: 
 136:   const expected = project ? resolveProjectWorktreeBranch(project) : DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
 137:   return normalized === expected
 138:     || normalized === DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH
 139:     || normalized.startsWith(`${DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH}/`)
 140: }
 141: 
 142: function gitCommonDir(cwd: string): string {
 143:   const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--git-common-dir'], {
 144:     cwd,
 145:   }).trim()
 146: 
 147:   if (!output) {
 148:     throw new Error(`Unable to resolve git common-dir for: ${cwd}`)
 149:   }
 150: 
 151:   return isAbsolute(output) ? resolve(output) : resolve(cwd, output)
 152: }
 153: 
 154: function branchExists(repoRoot: string, branch: string): boolean {
 155:   const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--verify', `refs/heads/${branch}`], {
 156:     cwd: repoRoot,
 157:     allowFailure: true,
 158:   })
 159:   return output.trim().length > 0
 160: }
 161: 
 162: function samePath(left: string, right: string): boolean {
 163:   return normalizePath(left) === normalizePath(right)
 164: }
 165: 
 166: function normalizePath(value: string): string {
 167:   let normalizedPath = resolve(value)
 168:   if (existsSync(normalizedPath)) {
 169:     try {
 170:       normalizedPath = realpathSync.native
 171:         ? realpathSync.native(normalizedPath)
 172:         : realpathSync(normalizedPath)
 173:     } catch {
 174:       // Fall back to the resolved path when realpath fails.
 175:     }
 176:   }
 177: 
 178:   const normalized = normalizedPath.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/+$/, '')
 179:   return platform === 'win32' ? normalized.toLowerCase() : normalized
 180: }

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
   1: import { copyFileSync, existsSync, lstatSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
   2: import { dirname, join, resolve } from 'node:path'
   3: 
   4: import type { ProjectEntry } from './config.js'
   5: 
   6: const DEV_TO_MEMORY_PATHS = [
   7:   'AGENTS.md',
   8:   'Memory/01_goals',
   9:   'Memory/02_todos',
  10:   'Memory/03_chat_logs',
  11:   'Memory/04_knowledge',
  12:   'Memory/05_archive',
  13: ] as const
  14: 
  15: const MEMORY_TO_DEV_PATHS = [
  16:   'Memory/06_transcripts',
  17:   'Memory/07_reports',
  18: ] as const
  19: 
  20: export interface SyncStats {
  21:   readonly copied: number
  22:   readonly deleted: number
  23:   readonly skipped: boolean
  24: }
  25: 
  26: export function projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath(project: ProjectEntry): boolean {
  27:   return normalizeComparablePath(project.development_path) !== normalizeComparablePath(project.memory_path)
  28: }
  29: 
  30: export function syncProjectContextToMemory(project: ProjectEntry): SyncStats {
  31:   if (!projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath(project)) {
  32:     return { copied: 0, deleted: 0, skipped: true }
  33:   }
  34: 
  35:   return syncPathSet(project.development_path, project.memory_path, DEV_TO_MEMORY_PATHS)
  36: }
  37: 
  38: export function syncProjectOutputsToDevelopment(project: ProjectEntry): SyncStats {
  39:   if (!projectUsesIsolatedMemoryPath(project)) {
  40:     return { copied: 0, deleted: 0, skipped: true }
  41:   }
  42: 
  43:   return syncPathSet(project.memory_path, project.development_path, MEMORY_TO_DEV_PATHS)
  44: }
  45: 
  46: function syncPathSet(
  47:   sourceRoot: string,
  48:   targetRoot: string,
  49:   relativePaths: readonly string[],
  50: ): SyncStats {
  51:   let copied = 0
  52:   let deleted = 0
  53: 
  54:   for (const relativePath of relativePaths) {
  55:     const stats = syncManagedPath(
  56:       resolve(sourceRoot, relativePath),
  57:       resolve(targetRoot, relativePath),
  58:     )
  59:     copied += stats.copied
  60:     deleted += stats.deleted
  61:   }
  62: 
  63:   return {
  64:     copied,
  65:     deleted,
  66:     skipped: false,
  67:   }
  68: }
  69: 
  70: function syncManagedPath(sourcePath: string, targetPath: string): Omit<SyncStats, 'skipped'> {
  71:   if (!existsSync(sourcePath)) {
  72:     return {
  73:       copied: 0,
  74:       deleted: removeTree(targetPath),
  75:     }
  76:   }
  77: 
  78:   const sourceStats = lstatSync(sourcePath)
  79:   if (sourceStats.isDirectory()) {
  80:     return syncDirectory(sourcePath, targetPath)
  81:   }
  82: 
  83:   mkdirSync(dirname(targetPath), { recursive: true })
  84:   if (existsSync(targetPath) && lstatSync(targetPath).isDirectory()) {
  85:     rmSync(targetPath, { recursive: true, force: true })
  86:   }
  87:   copyFileSync(sourcePath, targetPath)
  88:   return { copied: 1, deleted: 0 }
  89: }
  90: 
  91: function syncDirectory(sourceDir: string, targetDir: string): Omit<SyncStats, 'skipped'> {
  92:   if (existsSync(targetDir) && !lstatSync(targetDir).isDirectory()) {
  93:     rmSync(targetDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
  94:   }
  95: 
  96:   mkdirSync(targetDir, { recursive: true })
  97: 
  98:   let copied = 0
  99:   let deleted = 0
 100:   const sourceNames = new Set(readdirSync(sourceDir))
 101: 
 102:   for (const name of sourceNames) {
 103:     const stats = syncManagedPath(join(sourceDir, name), join(targetDir, name))
 104:     copied += stats.copied
 105:     deleted += stats.deleted
 106:   }
 107: 
 108:   for (const name of readdirSync(targetDir)) {
 109:     if (sourceNames.has(name)) continue
 110:     deleted += removeTree(join(targetDir, name))
 111:   }
 112: 
 113:   return { copied, deleted }
 114: }
 115: 
 116: function removeTree(targetPath: string): number {
 117:   if (!existsSync(targetPath)) {
 118:     return 0
 119:   }
 120: 
 121:   const count = countFiles(targetPath)
 122:   rmSync(targetPath, { recursive: true, force: true })
 123:   return count
 124: }
 125: 
 126: function countFiles(targetPath: string): number {
 127:   const stats = lstatSync(targetPath)
 128:   if (!stats.isDirectory()) {
 129:     return 1
 130:   }
 131: 
 132:   let total = 0
 133:   for (const name of readdirSync(targetPath)) {
 134:     total += countFiles(join(targetPath, name))
 135:   }
 136:   return total
 137: }
 138: 
 139: function normalizeComparablePath(value: string): string {
 140:   return resolve(value).replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/+$/, '')
 141: }

Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
   1: /**
   2:  * Load, validate, and manage ~/.memorytree/config.toml.
   3:  * Port of scripts/_config_utils.py
   4:  */
   5: 
   6: import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
   7: import { homedir } from 'node:os'
   8: import { basename, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'
   9: 
  10: import { parse as parseToml } from 'smol-toml'
  11: 
  12: import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
  13: 
  14: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  15: // Constants
  16: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  17: 
  18: const DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = '5m'
  19: const DEFAULT_REFRESH_INTERVAL = '30m'
  20: export const DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH = 'memorytree'
  21: const DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH = true
  22: const DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL = 'info'
  23: const DEFAULT_GENERATE_REPORT = false
  24: const DEFAULT_AI_SUMMARY_MODEL = 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'
  25: const DEFAULT_LOCALE = 'en'
  26: const DEFAULT_GH_PAGES_BRANCH = ''
  27: const DEFAULT_CNAME = ''
  28: const DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_URL = ''
  29: const DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL = ''
  30: const DEFAULT_REPORT_PORT = 10010
  31: const DEFAULT_REPORT_EXPOSURE = 'local'
  32: const VALID_LOG_LEVELS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error'])
  33: 
  34: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  35: // Types
  36: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  37: 
  38: export type ReportExposure = 'local' | 'lan'
  39: 
  40: export interface ProjectEntry {
  41:   readonly id: string
  42:   readonly path: string
  43:   readonly name: string
  44:   readonly development_path: string
  45:   readonly memory_path: string
  46:   readonly memory_branch: string
  47:   readonly heartbeat_interval: string
  48:   readonly refresh_interval: string
  49:   readonly auto_push: boolean
  50:   readonly generate_report: boolean
  51:   readonly ai_summary_model: string
  52:   readonly locale: string
  53:   readonly gh_pages_branch: string
  54:   readonly cname: string
  55:   readonly webhook_url: string
  56:   readonly report_base_url: string
  57:   readonly report_port: number
  58:   readonly report_exposure: ReportExposure
  59:   readonly last_heartbeat_at: string
  60:   readonly last_refresh_at: string
  61: }
  62: 
  63: export interface Config {
  64:   readonly heartbeat_interval: string
  65:   readonly watch_dirs: readonly string[]
  66:   readonly projects: readonly ProjectEntry[]
  67:   readonly auto_push: boolean
  68:   readonly log_level: string
  69:   readonly generate_report: boolean
  70:   readonly ai_summary_model: string
  71:   readonly locale: string
  72:   readonly gh_pages_branch: string
  73:   readonly cname: string
  74:   readonly webhook_url: string
  75:   /** Base URL for RSS/OG meta (e.g. 'https://memory.example.com'). Empty = skip. */
  76:   readonly report_base_url: string
  77:   /** Port for the report HTTP server. Default: 10010. */
  78:   readonly report_port: number
  79:   /** Exposure for local report hosting. Default: local. */
  80:   readonly report_exposure: ReportExposure
  81: }
  82: 
  83: type ProjectLike = Partial<ProjectEntry> & {
  84:   readonly path?: string
  85:   readonly name?: string
  86:   readonly development_path?: string
  87:   readonly memory_path?: string
  88: }
  89: 
  90: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  91: // Path helpers
  92: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  93: 
  94: export function memorytreeRoot(): string {
  95:   return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree')
  96: }
  97: 
  98: export function configPath(): string {
  99:   return resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'config.toml')
 100: }
 101: 
 102: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 103: // Load / Save
 104: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 105: 
 106: export function loadConfig(): Config {
 107:   const path = configPath()
 108:   if (!existsSync(path)) {
 109:     return defaultConfig()
 110:   }
 111: 
 112:   let raw: Record<string, unknown>
 113:   try {
 114:     const text = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
 115:     raw = parseToml(text) as Record<string, unknown>
 116:   } catch {
 117:     return defaultConfig()
 118:   }
 119: 
 120:   return parseRaw(raw)
 121: }
 122: 
 123: export function saveConfig(cfg: Config): void {
 124:   const normalized = normalizeConfig(cfg)
 125:   const path = configPath()
 126:   mkdirSync(dirname(path), { recursive: true })
 127: 
 128:   const lines: string[] = [
 129:     `heartbeat_interval = ${tomlString(normalized.heartbeat_interval)}`,
 130:     `auto_push = ${normalized.auto_push ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
 131:     `log_level = ${tomlString(normalized.log_level)}`,
 132:     `generate_report = ${normalized.generate_report ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
 133:     `ai_summary_model = ${tomlString(normalized.ai_summary_model)}`,
 134:     `locale = ${tomlString(normalized.locale)}`,
 135:     `gh_pages_branch = ${tomlString(normalized.gh_pages_branch)}`,
 136:     `cname = ${tomlString(normalized.cname)}`,
 137:     `webhook_url = ${tomlString(normalized.webhook_url)}`,
 138:     `report_base_url = ${tomlString(normalized.report_base_url)}`,
 139:     `report_port = ${normalized.report_port}`,
 140:     `report_exposure = ${tomlString(normalized.report_exposure)}`,
 141:   ]
 142: 
 143:   if (normalized.watch_dirs.length > 0) {
 144:     const items = normalized.watch_dirs.map(dir => tomlString(dir)).join(', ')
 145:     lines.push(`watch_dirs = [${items}]`)
 146:   } else {
 147:     lines.push('watch_dirs = []')
 148:   }
 149: 
 150:   lines.push('')
 151:   for (const project of normalized.projects) {
 152:     lines.push('[[projects]]')
 153:     lines.push(`id = ${tomlString(project.id)}`)
 154:     lines.push(`path = ${tomlString(project.path)}`)
 155:     lines.push(`name = ${tomlString(project.name)}`)
 156:     lines.push(`development_path = ${tomlString(project.development_path)}`)
 157:     lines.push(`memory_path = ${tomlString(project.memory_path)}`)
 158:     lines.push(`memory_branch = ${tomlString(project.memory_branch)}`)
 159:     lines.push(`heartbeat_interval = ${tomlString(project.heartbeat_interval)}`)
 160:     lines.push(`refresh_interval = ${tomlString(project.refresh_interval)}`)
 161:     lines.push(`auto_push = ${project.auto_push ? 'true' : 'false'}`)
 162:     lines.push(`generate_report = ${project.generate_report ? 'true' : 'false'}`)
 163:     lines.push(`ai_summary_model = ${tomlString(project.ai_summary_model)}`)
 164:     lines.push(`locale = ${tomlString(project.locale)}`)
 165:     lines.push(`gh_pages_branch = ${tomlString(project.gh_pages_branch)}`)
 166:     lines.push(`cname = ${tomlString(project.cname)}`)
 167:     lines.push(`webhook_url = ${tomlString(project.webhook_url)}`)
 168:     lines.push(`report_base_url = ${tomlString(project.report_base_url)}`)
 169:     lines.push(`report_port = ${project.report_port}`)
 170:     lines.push(`report_exposure = ${tomlString(project.report_exposure)}`)
 171:     if (project.last_heartbeat_at) {
 172:       lines.push(`last_heartbeat_at = ${tomlString(project.last_heartbeat_at)}`)
 173:     }
 174:     if (project.last_refresh_at) {
 175:       lines.push(`last_refresh_at = ${tomlString(project.last_refresh_at)}`)
 176:     }
 177:     lines.push('')
 178:   }
 179: 
 180:   writeFileSync(path, lines.join('\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
 181: }
 182: 
 183: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 184: // Interval parsing
 185: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 186: 
 187: export function intervalToSeconds(interval: string): number {
 188:   const match = interval.trim().toLowerCase().match(/^(\d+)\s*(s|m|h)$/)
 189:   if (!match) {
 190:     return intervalToSeconds(DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
 191:   }
 192:   const value = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)
 193:   const unit = match[2]!
 194:   if (value <= 0) {
 195:     return intervalToSeconds(DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
 196:   }
 197:   const multiplier: Record<string, number> = { s: 1, m: 60, h: 3600 }
 198:   return value * (multiplier[unit] ?? 60)
 199: }
 200: 
 201: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 202: // Project helpers
 203: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 204: 
 205: export function registerProject(
 206:   cfg: Config,
 207:   repoPath: string,
 208:   overrides: Partial<ProjectLike> = {},
 209: ): Config {
 210:   const normalized = normalizeConfig(cfg)
 211:   const resolved = normalizePath(repoPath)
 212:   for (const entry of normalized.projects) {
 213:     if (projectContainsPath(entry, resolved)) {
 214:       return normalized
 215:     }
 216:   }
 217: 
 218:   const projectInput: Record<string, unknown> = {
 219:     path: overrides.path ?? resolved,
 220:     development_path: overrides.development_path ?? resolved,
 221:     memory_path: overrides.memory_path ?? resolved,
 222:   }
 223:   if (overrides.name !== undefined) projectInput.name = overrides.name
 224:   if (overrides.id !== undefined) projectInput.id = overrides.id
 225:   if (overrides.heartbeat_interval !== undefined) projectInput.heartbeat_interval = overrides.heartbeat_interval
 226:   if (overrides.refresh_interval !== undefined) projectInput.refresh_interval = overrides.refresh_interval
 227:   if (overrides.memory_branch !== undefined) projectInput.memory_branch = overrides.memory_branch
 228:   if (overrides.auto_push !== undefined) projectInput.auto_push = overrides.auto_push
 229:   if (overrides.generate_report !== undefined) projectInput.generate_report = overrides.generate_report
 230:   if (overrides.ai_summary_model !== undefined) projectInput.ai_summary_model = overrides.ai_summary_model
 231:   if (overrides.locale !== undefined) projectInput.locale = overrides.locale
 232:   if (overrides.gh_pages_branch !== undefined) projectInput.gh_pages_branch = overrides.gh_pages_branch
 233:   if (overrides.cname !== undefined) projectInput.cname = overrides.cname
 234:   if (overrides.webhook_url !== undefined) projectInput.webhook_url = overrides.webhook_url
 235:   if (overrides.report_base_url !== undefined) projectInput.report_base_url = overrides.report_base_url
 236:   if (overrides.report_port !== undefined) projectInput.report_port = overrides.report_port
 237:   if (overrides.report_exposure !== undefined) projectInput.report_exposure = overrides.report_exposure
 238:   if (overrides.last_heartbeat_at !== undefined) projectInput.last_heartbeat_at = overrides.last_heartbeat_at
 239: 
 240:   const project = normalizeProjectEntry(projectInput as ProjectLike, normalized)
 241: 
 242:   return {
 243:     ...normalized,
 244:     projects: [...normalized.projects, project],
 245:   }
 246: }
 247: 
 248: export function upsertProject(
 249:   cfg: Config,
 250:   repoPath: string,
 251:   overrides: Partial<ProjectLike> = {},
 252: ): Config {
 253:   const normalized = normalizeConfig(cfg)
 254:   const resolved = normalizePath(repoPath)
 255:   const existing = findProjectForPath(normalized, resolved)
 256:   if (existing === null) {
 257:     return registerProject(normalized, repoPath, overrides)
 258:   }
 259: 
 260:   const project = normalizeProjectEntry({
 261:     ...existing,
 262:     ...overrides,
 263:     development_path: overrides.development_path ?? existing.development_path,
 264:     memory_path: overrides.memory_path ?? existing.memory_path,
 265:     memory_branch: overrides.memory_branch ?? existing.memory_branch,
 266:     path: overrides.path ?? existing.path,
 267:   }, normalized)
 268: 
 269:   return {
 270:     ...normalized,
 271:     projects: normalized.projects.map(entry => (
 272:       entry.id === existing.id ? project : entry
 273:     )),
 274:   }
 275: }
 276: 
 277: export function findProjectForPath(config: Config, candidatePath: string): ProjectEntry | null {
 278:   const normalized = normalizePath(candidatePath)
 279:   let bestMatch: ProjectEntry | null = null
 280:   let bestLength = -1
 281: 
 282:   for (const entry of config.projects) {
 283:     for (const root of projectRoots(entry)) {
 284:       if (!pathContains(root, normalized)) continue
 285:       if (root.length > bestLength) {
 286:         bestMatch = entry
 287:         bestLength = root.length
 288:       }
 289:     }
 290:   }
 291: 
 292:   return bestMatch
 293: }
 294: 
 295: export function resolveReportPort(config: Config, candidatePath: string): number {
 296:   const project = findProjectForPath(config, candidatePath)
 297:   return project?.report_port ?? config.report_port
 298: }
 299: 
 300: export function resolveReportExposure(config: Config, candidatePath: string): ReportExposure {
 301:   const project = findProjectForPath(config, candidatePath)
 302:   return project?.report_exposure ?? config.report_exposure
 303: }
 304: 
 305: export function projectExecutionPath(project: ProjectEntry): string {
 306:   return normalizePath(project.memory_path || project.path || project.development_path)
 307: }
 308: 
 309: export function projectDisplayName(project: ProjectEntry): string {
 310:   return project.name || basename(project.development_path || project.path || project.memory_path)
 311: }
 312: 
 313: export function projectIsDue(project: ProjectEntry, now: Date = new Date()): boolean {
 314:   if (!project.last_heartbeat_at) return true
 315: 
 316:   const lastRunMs = Date.parse(project.last_heartbeat_at)
 317:   if (!Number.isFinite(lastRunMs)) return true
 318: 
 319:   const dueAfterMs = intervalToSeconds(project.heartbeat_interval) * 1000
 320:   return now.getTime() - lastRunMs >= dueAfterMs
 321: }
 322: 
 323: export function projectRefreshIsDue(project: ProjectEntry, now: Date = new Date()): boolean {
 324:   if (!project.last_refresh_at) return true
 325: 
 326:   const lastRefreshMs = Date.parse(project.last_refresh_at)
 327:   if (!Number.isFinite(lastRefreshMs)) return true
 328: 
 329:   const dueAfterMs = intervalToSeconds(project.refresh_interval) * 1000
 330:   return now.getTime() - lastRefreshMs >= dueAfterMs
 331: }
 332: 
 333: export function noteProjectHeartbeatRun(config: Config, projectId: string, at: string): Config {
 334:   const normalized = normalizeConfig(config)
 335:   return {
 336:     ...normalized,
 337:     projects: normalized.projects.map(project => (
 338:       project.id === projectId
 339:         ? { ...project, last_heartbeat_at: at }
 340:         : project
 341:     )),
 342:   }
 343: }
 344: 
 345: export function noteProjectRefreshRun(config: Config, projectId: string, at: string): Config {
 346:   const normalized = normalizeConfig(config)
 347:   return {
 348:     ...normalized,
 349:     projects: normalized.projects.map(project => (
 350:       project.id === projectId
 351:         ? { ...project, last_refresh_at: at }
 352:         : project
 353:     )),
 354:   }
 355: }
 356: 
 357: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 358: // Internal helpers
 359: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 360: 
 361: function defaultConfig(): Config {
 362:   return {
 363:     heartbeat_interval: DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
 364:     watch_dirs: [],
 365:     projects: [],
 366:     auto_push: DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH,
 367:     log_level: DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL,
 368:     generate_report: DEFAULT_GENERATE_REPORT,
 369:     ai_summary_model: DEFAULT_AI_SUMMARY_MODEL,
 370:     locale: DEFAULT_LOCALE,
 371:     gh_pages_branch: DEFAULT_GH_PAGES_BRANCH,
 372:     cname: DEFAULT_CNAME,
 373:     webhook_url: DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_URL,
 374:     report_base_url: DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL,
 375:     report_port: DEFAULT_REPORT_PORT,
 376:     report_exposure: DEFAULT_REPORT_EXPOSURE,
 377:   }
 378: }
 379: 
 380: function parseRaw(raw: Record<string, unknown>): Config {
 381:   const interval = typeof raw['heartbeat_interval'] === 'string' && isValidInterval(raw['heartbeat_interval'])
 382:     ? raw['heartbeat_interval']
 383:     : DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
 384: 
 385:   const autoPush = typeof raw['auto_push'] === 'boolean'
 386:     ? raw['auto_push']
 387:     : DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH
 388: 
 389:   const rawLogLevel = raw['log_level']
 390:   const logLevel = typeof rawLogLevel === 'string' && VALID_LOG_LEVELS.has(rawLogLevel.toLowerCase())
 391:     ? rawLogLevel.toLowerCase()
 392:     : DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL
 393: 
 394:   const watchDirs: string[] = []
 395:   const rawDirs = raw['watch_dirs']
 396:   if (Array.isArray(rawDirs)) {
 397:     for (const dir of rawDirs) {
 398:       if (typeof dir === 'string') {
 399:         watchDirs.push(dir)
 400:       }
 401:     }
 402:   }
 403: 
 404:   const generateReport = typeof raw['generate_report'] === 'boolean'
 405:     ? raw['generate_report']
 406:     : DEFAULT_GENERATE_REPORT
 407: 
 408:   const aiSummaryModel = typeof raw['ai_summary_model'] === 'string' && raw['ai_summary_model']
 409:     ? raw['ai_summary_model']
 410:     : DEFAULT_AI_SUMMARY_MODEL
 411: 
 412:   const locale = typeof raw['locale'] === 'string' && raw['locale']
 413:     ? raw['locale']
 414:     : DEFAULT_LOCALE
 415: 
 416:   const ghPagesBranch = typeof raw['gh_pages_branch'] === 'string'
 417:     ? raw['gh_pages_branch']
 418:     : DEFAULT_GH_PAGES_BRANCH
 419: 
 420:   const cname = typeof raw['cname'] === 'string'
 421:     ? raw['cname']
 422:     : DEFAULT_CNAME
 423: 
 424:   const webhookUrl = typeof raw['webhook_url'] === 'string'
 425:     ? raw['webhook_url']
 426:     : DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_URL
 427: 
 428:   const reportBaseUrl = typeof raw['report_base_url'] === 'string'
 429:     ? raw['report_base_url']
 430:     : DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL
 431: 
 432:   const reportPort = isValidPort(raw['report_port'])
 433:     ? raw['report_port']
 434:     : DEFAULT_REPORT_PORT
 435:   const reportExposure = normalizeReportExposure(raw['report_exposure'], DEFAULT_REPORT_EXPOSURE)
 436: 
 437:   const cfg: Config = {
 438:     heartbeat_interval: interval,
 439:     watch_dirs: watchDirs,
 440:     projects: [],
 441:     auto_push: autoPush,
 442:     log_level: logLevel,
 443:     generate_report: generateReport,
 444:     ai_summary_model: aiSummaryModel,
 445:     locale,
 446:     gh_pages_branch: ghPagesBranch,
 447:     cname,
 448:     webhook_url: webhookUrl,
 449:     report_base_url: reportBaseUrl,
 450:     report_port: reportPort,
 451:     report_exposure: reportExposure,
 452:   }
 453: 
 454:   const projects: ProjectEntry[] = []
 455:   const rawProjects = raw['projects']
 456:   if (Array.isArray(rawProjects)) {
 457:     for (const entry of rawProjects) {
 458:       if (entry === null || typeof entry !== 'object' || Array.isArray(entry)) continue
 459:       try {
 460:         const normalized = normalizeProjectEntry(entry as ProjectLike, cfg)
 461:         projects.push(normalized)
 462:       } catch {
 463:         continue
 464:       }
 465:     }
 466:   }
 467: 
 468:   return {
 469:     ...cfg,
 470:     projects,
 471:   }
 472: }
 473: 
 474: function normalizeConfig(cfg: Config): Config {
 475:   const normalizedBase: Config = {
 476:     heartbeat_interval: isValidInterval(cfg.heartbeat_interval) ? cfg.heartbeat_interval : DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
 477:     watch_dirs: [...cfg.watch_dirs].filter((dir): dir is string => typeof dir === 'string'),
 478:     projects: [],
 479:     auto_push: typeof cfg.auto_push === 'boolean' ? cfg.auto_push : DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH,
 480:     log_level: isValidLogLevel(cfg.log_level) ? cfg.log_level.toLowerCase() : DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL,
 481:     generate_report: typeof cfg.generate_report === 'boolean' ? cfg.generate_report : DEFAULT_GENERATE_REPORT,
 482:     ai_summary_model: nonEmptyString(cfg.ai_summary_model, DEFAULT_AI_SUMMARY_MODEL),
 483:     locale: nonEmptyString(cfg.locale, DEFAULT_LOCALE),
 484:     gh_pages_branch: stringOrDefault(cfg.gh_pages_branch, DEFAULT_GH_PAGES_BRANCH),
 485:     cname: stringOrDefault(cfg.cname, DEFAULT_CNAME),
 486:     webhook_url: stringOrDefault(cfg.webhook_url, DEFAULT_WEBHOOK_URL),
 487:     report_base_url: stringOrDefault(cfg.report_base_url, DEFAULT_REPORT_BASE_URL),
 488:     report_port: isValidPort(cfg.report_port) ? cfg.report_port : DEFAULT_REPORT_PORT,
 489:     report_exposure: normalizeReportExposure(cfg.report_exposure, DEFAULT_REPORT_EXPOSURE),
 490:   }
 491: 
 492:   const projects = cfg.projects.map(project => normalizeProjectEntry(project, normalizedBase))
 493:   return {
 494:     ...normalizedBase,
 495:     projects,
 496:   }
 497: }
 498: 
 499: function normalizeProjectEntry(project: ProjectLike, cfg: Config): ProjectEntry {
 500:   const rawPath = maybeNormalizePath(project.path)
 501:   const developmentPath = maybeNormalizePath(project.development_path) || rawPath
 502:   const memoryPath = maybeNormalizePath(project.memory_path) || rawPath || developmentPath
 503:   const canonicalPath = memoryPath || rawPath || developmentPath
 504: 
 505:   if (!canonicalPath) {
 506:     throw new Error('Project entry requires at least one path.')
 507:   }
 508: 
 509:   const resolvedDevelopmentPath = developmentPath || canonicalPath
 510:   const resolvedMemoryPath = memoryPath || canonicalPath
 511:   const name = nonEmptyString(project.name, basename(resolvedDevelopmentPath))
 512:   const id = createProjectId(
 513:     typeof project.id === 'string' ? project.id : '',
 514:     resolvedDevelopmentPath,
 515:     resolvedMemoryPath,
 516:   )
 517: 
 518:   return {
 519:     id,
 520:     path: canonicalPath,
 521:     name,
 522:     development_path: resolvedDevelopmentPath,
 523:     memory_path: resolvedMemoryPath,
 524:     memory_branch: nonEmptyString(project.memory_branch, DEFAULT_MEMORY_BRANCH),
 525:     heartbeat_interval: isValidInterval(project.heartbeat_interval ?? '') ? project.heartbeat_interval! : cfg.heartbeat_interval,
 526:     refresh_interval: isValidInterval(project.refresh_interval ?? '') ? project.refresh_interval! : DEFAULT_REFRESH_INTERVAL,
 527:     auto_push: typeof project.auto_push === 'boolean' ? project.auto_push : cfg.auto_push,
 528:     generate_report: typeof project.generate_report === 'boolean' ? project.generate_report : cfg.generate_report,
 529:     ai_summary_model: nonEmptyString(project.ai_summary_model, cfg.ai_summary_model),
 530:     locale: nonEmptyString(project.locale, cfg.locale),
 531:     gh_pages_branch: stringOrDefault(project.gh_pages_branch, cfg.gh_pages_branch),
 532:     cname: stringOrDefault(project.cname, cfg.cname),
 533:     webhook_url: stringOrDefault(project.webhook_url, cfg.webhook_url),
 534:     report_base_url: stringOrDefault(project.report_base_url, cfg.report_base_url),
 535:     report_port: isValidPort(project.report_port) ? project.report_port : cfg.report_port,
 536:     report_exposure: normalizeReportExposure(project.report_exposure, cfg.report_exposure),
 537:     last_heartbeat_at: isValidIsoTimestamp(project.last_heartbeat_at) ? project.last_heartbeat_at : '',
 538:     last_refresh_at: isValidIsoTimestamp(project.last_refresh_at) ? project.last_refresh_at : '',
 539:   }
 540: }
 541: 
 542: function isValidInterval(value: string): boolean {
 543:   const match = value.trim().toLowerCase().match(/^(\d+)\s*(s|m|h)$/)
 544:   if (!match) return false
 545:   return parseInt(match[1]!, 10) > 0
 546: }
 547: 
 548: function isValidPort(value: unknown): value is number {
 549:   return typeof value === 'number'
 550:     && Number.isInteger(value)
 551:     && value > 0
 552:     && value <= 65535
 553: }
 554: 
 555: function normalizeReportExposure(value: unknown, fallback: ReportExposure): ReportExposure {
 556:   if (typeof value !== 'string') return fallback
 557:   const normalized = value.trim().toLowerCase()
 558:   return normalized === 'lan' ? 'lan' : normalized === 'local' ? 'local' : fallback
 559: }
 560: 
 561: function isValidIsoTimestamp(value: unknown): value is string {
 562:   return typeof value === 'string'
 563:     && value.length > 0
 564:     && Number.isFinite(Date.parse(value))
 565: }
 566: 
 567: function isValidLogLevel(value: unknown): value is string {
 568:   return typeof value === 'string' && VALID_LOG_LEVELS.has(value.toLowerCase())
 569: }
 570: 
 571: function nonEmptyString(value: unknown, fallback: string): string {
 572:   return typeof value === 'string' && value ? value : fallback
 573: }
 574: 
 575: function stringOrDefault(value: unknown, fallback: string): string {
 576:   return typeof value === 'string' ? value : fallback
 577: }
 578: 
 579: function maybeNormalizePath(value: unknown): string {
 580:   return typeof value === 'string' && value
 581:     ? normalizePath(value)
 582:     : ''
 583: }
 584: 
 585: function normalizePath(value: string): string {
 586:   if (value.startsWith('/')) {
 587:     return toPosixPath(value)
 588:   }
 589:   if (/^[a-zA-Z]:[\\/]/.test(value) || value.startsWith('\\\\')) {
 590:     return toPosixPath(resolve(value))
 591:   }
 592:   return toPosixPath(resolve(value))
 593: }
 594: 
 595: function createProjectId(requestedId: string, developmentPath: string, memoryPath: string): string {
 596:   const source = requestedId || `${developmentPath}--${memoryPath}`
 597:   const normalized = source
 598:     .toLowerCase()
 599:     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
 600:     .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
 601:     .replace(/-{2,}/g, '-')
 602: 
 603:   return normalized || 'project'
 604: }
 605: 
 606: function projectRoots(project: ProjectEntry): string[] {
 607:   return [...new Set([
 608:     normalizePath(project.development_path),
 609:     normalizePath(project.memory_path),
 610:     normalizePath(project.path),
 611:   ])]
 612: }
 613: 
 614: function projectContainsPath(project: ProjectEntry, candidatePath: string): boolean {
 615:   return projectRoots(project).some(root => pathContains(root, candidatePath))
 616: }
 617: 
 618: function pathContains(root: string, candidate: string): boolean {
 619:   if (candidate === root) return true
 620:   const prefix = root.endsWith('/') ? root : `${root}/`
